When `singlePageMode = true` (i.e., `targetParseable == maxWalk`), the true-branch calls `getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, targetParseable)` and the false-branch calls `getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk)` — but at that point `targetParseable == maxWalk`, so both calls are identical. The `if/else` can be collapsed to a single unconditional call `getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk)`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Remove the `singlePageMode` variable and the `if/else` block. Replace with a single call: `val buildsResult = bambooService.getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk)`. Remove the corresponding KDoc paragraph explaining the distinction, which is also no longer accurate.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading TagBuilderService.kt lines 90-99. The condition val singlePageMode = targetParseable == maxWalk guards a branch that calls getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, targetParseable) vs getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk). When singlePageMode=true, targetParseable == maxWalk, so both arguments are identical — the if/else is a no-op distinction. The real pagination difference is in the early-exit logic at line 179 (if ranked.size >= targetParseable break), not in the fetch call. Collapsing to a single unconditional getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk) is safe. The KDoc explaining the legacy single-page mode as a distinct fetch is misleading and should be simplified.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Remove the singlePageMode variable and the if/else block, replacing with a single call: val buildsResult = bambooService.getRecentBuilds(suitePlanKey, maxWalk). Update the KDoc paragraph that incorrectly explains the if/else as a meaningful distinction. No functional change.

confirmed.title: CurrentRepoContext.branchName and .detectedFrom are struct fields never read in production
confirmed.description: `CurrentRepoContext` carries two fields — `branchName: String` (line 104) and `detectedFrom: DetectionSource` (line 106) — that are never read by any production consumer. `TagBuilderService.replaceCurrentRepoTag()` only accesses `context.serviceName` and `context.featureBranchTag`. All three call sites in `AutomationPanel` pass `focusBuild.branch` and `DetectionSource.SETTINGS_MAPPING` but these values go nowhere. The `DetectionSource` enum's values `PROJECT_NAME` and `GIT_BRANCH` are never used in production (only `SETTINGS_MAPPING` is passed, and even that is dropped).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Remove `branchName` and `detectedFrom` from `CurrentRepoContext`. Simplify the data class to `data class CurrentRepoContext(val serviceName: String, val featureBranchTag: String?)`. Remove `DetectionSource` enum entirely. Update the three `AutomationPanel` call sites and the two test cases that construct `CurrentRepoContext` with 4 arguments.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading TagBuilderService.kt lines 257-274: replaceCurrentRepoTag only accesses context.serviceName and context.featureBranchTag. The branchName and detectedFrom fields are never read in any production code path. All three AutomationPanel call sites (lines 561-565, 644-648, 816-820) pass focusBuild.branch and DetectionSource.SETTINGS_MAPPING but these values are carried by the struct and never consumed. DetectionSource.PROJECT_NAME and GIT_BRANCH are never used as values anywhere in production (only SETTINGS_MAPPING is passed, itself ignored). AutomationModelsTest line 108-116 tests CurrentRepoContext with all 4 args. Removing branchName and detectedFrom is safe — no consumer reads them.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Remove branchName and detectedFrom from CurrentRepoContext data class. Remove DetectionSource enum entirely. Update the three AutomationPanel call sites (remove the two dead args) and update AutomationModelsTest to drop the DetectionSource field. Low priority cleanup.

confirmed.title: Direct cross-module dependency on :bamboo UI package
confirmed.description: AutomationPanel imports and directly instantiates `ManualStageDialog` and uses `TriggerMode` from `com.workflow.orchestrator.bamboo.ui` — a sibling feature module. The project convention is that feature modules depend ONLY on `:core`; cross-module interaction must go through `:core` interfaces or EventBus. The `:automation` build.gradle.kts already lists `implementation(project(":bamboo"))`, making the compile-level coupling explicit and creating a directed dependency cycle risk if `:bamboo` ever needs `:automation` types.
confirmed.evidence:
import com.workflow.orchestrator.bamboo.ui.ManualStageDialog
import com.workflow.orchestrator.bamboo.ui.TriggerMode
...
val dialog = ManualStageDialog(project = project, planKey = currentSuitePlanKey, scope = scope, triggerMode = TriggerMode.CUSTOM_STAGES, ...)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Move `ManualStageDialog` and `TriggerMode` to `:core` (e.g. `core/ui/bamboo/` or `core/model/`), or define a `StagePickerLauncher` extension point in `:core` (similar to `createPrLauncher` / `openPrLister`) that `:bamboo` registers and `:automation` calls through the EP. Either approach severs the direct `:automation → :bamboo` compile dependency and lets the build.gradle.kts drop `implementation(project(":bamboo"))` entirely.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: AutomationPanel.kt lines 25-26 import com.workflow.orchestrator.bamboo.ui.ManualStageDialog and TriggerMode. Lines 1002-1009 instantiate ManualStageDialog directly. The automation/build.gradle.kts line 75 declares implementation(project(':bamboo')). The bamboo module has no dependency on automation (no output from reverse grep), so there is no cycle yet, but the :automation -> :bamboo compile dependency violates the stated convention that feature modules depend ONLY on :core. This is a real architectural violation. ManualStageDialog is defined in the bamboo.ui package (not core), and TriggerMode is co-located there. Fixing requires either moving ManualStageDialog/TriggerMode to :core or introducing an extension-point seam in :core.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Move ManualStageDialog and TriggerMode to :core (e.g., core/ui/bamboo/ subpackage) or define a StagePicker extension point in :core that :bamboo registers and :automation invokes. Either approach removes the direct :automation -> :bamboo compile dependency. Update build.gradle.kts to drop implementation(project(':bamboo')). Medium effort; prioritize to avoid the dependency creeping further.

confirmed.title: dockerTagsJson content partially logged at INFO — information-disclosure risk
confirmed.description: Line 149 logs `dockerTagsJson.take(100)` at `log.info` level when the parsed JSON is empty. The existing code comment at line 279 (`buildJsonPayload`) explicitly bans logging payload content ('Full service→tag mappings may include internal registry paths and version fingerprints'). The take(100) truncation is insufficient: a single JSON entry like `{"myservice":"repo.internal.corp/svc:1.2.3-build.4567"}` is 57 chars, well within 100, leaking both the internal registry hostname and the Docker image reference into `idea.log` on every build where the parse fails (e.g. the first few builds of a plan before dockerTagsAsJson appears).
confirmed.evidence:
val reason = "#${build.buildNumber}: dockerTagsAsJson parsed to empty — ${dockerTagsJson.take(100)}"
log.info("[Automation:Tags]   $reason")
skippedReasons.add(reason)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace `${dockerTagsJson.take(100)}` with a non-content diagnostic: log the raw string length and a schema hint instead, e.g. `"dockerTagsAsJson was ${dockerTagsJson.length} chars, did not parse as a non-empty JSON object"`. This matches the approach already taken in `buildJsonPayload` (logs `entries.size` and `length`, never the content). The same fix should be applied to the `skippedReasons` string that is later surfaced in the diagnostic banner — the banner text will leak into the UI otherwise.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading TagBuilderService.kt line 149: the reason string logged at INFO is '#${build.buildNumber}: dockerTagsAsJson parsed to empty — ${dockerTagsJson.take(100)}'. The take(100) truncation does not prevent leaking internal registry hostnames or Docker image references — a typical entry like {'myservice':'repo.internal.corp/svc:1.2.3-build.4567'} is ~57 chars, well within 100. This contradicts the explicit project policy documented at line 279 in the same file: 'Log metadata only — never log payload content'. The same content is added to skippedReasons (line 151) which is surfaced in the UI diagnostic banner. The fix (log length and schema hint instead of content) matches the existing pattern in buildJsonPayload (lines 279-282) and has no risk of breaking functionality.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Replace '${dockerTagsJson.take(100)}' at line 149 with a non-content diagnostic such as 'dockerTagsAsJson was ${dockerTagsJson.length} chars, did not parse as a non-empty JSON object'. Apply the same replacement to the skippedReasons string to prevent leaking content into the UI banner. This matches the approach already used in buildJsonPayload.

confirmed.title: UI panels own manually-allocated CoroutineScope instead of using platform-injected scope
confirmed.description: Four UI classes — `AutomationPanel` (line 57), `MonitorPanel` (line 53), `QueueStatusPanel` (line 44), and `AutomationStatusBarWidgetFactory`'s inner widget (line 33) — each allocate their own `CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO + SupervisorJob())`. The `:core` CLAUDE.md and phase-4 conventions explicitly deprecate this pattern for `@Service` classes in favour of the platform-injected `cs: CoroutineScope`. UI panels are not `@Service` instances, so the impact is lower, but creating four independent `SupervisorJob` roots means any unhandled exception in one coroutine is silently swallowed by the Job (the `launchWithErrorSurface` wrapper in `QueueService` is absent in the panel scopes), and cancellation ordering is ad-hoc (`scope.cancel()` in `dispose()`). A leak in `dispose()` not being called (e.g. if `Disposer.register` was omitted for a child panel) would permanently prevent GC of the scope's coroutines.
confirmed.suggestedFix: For non-`@Service` Disposable UI panels the accepted IntelliJ 2024.1+ pattern is `cs.childScope()` or to obtain the project's `@Service`-injected scope via a thin `@Service`-level bridge (e.g. have a `@Service` own the scope and let the panel call `cs.launch { }`). At minimum, check that every panel that allocates its own scope has a matching `Disposer.register` so `dispose()` is always called, and add a top-level `CoroutineExceptionHandler` so exceptions in fire-and-forget coroutines surface in logs.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: AutomationPanel.kt:57, MonitorPanel.kt:53, QueueStatusPanel.kt:44, and AutomationStatusBarWidgetFactory.kt:33 each allocate their own CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO + SupervisorJob()). However, the finding overstates the leak risk. All panels properly call scope.cancel() in their dispose() methods (AutomationPanel:1166, MonitorPanel:773, QueueStatusPanel:170, status bar widget:85). AutomationPanel registers all child panels via Disposer.register at lines 356-359, and the tool-window factory calls content.setDisposer(panel) (WorkflowToolWindowFactory.kt:325-326, 346, 367) so the top-level dispose cascade is wired. The AutomationStatusBarWidgetFactory inner widget extends EditorBasedWidget which is Disposable managed by the platform. No actual lifecycle leak exists in the current code. The gap is a style/convention issue (not following the platform-injected cs pattern for @Service classes per core CLAUDE.md) but panels are not @Service classes — the convention docs explicitly say 'Non-@Service classes consolidate fire-and-forget launches onto a single field scope.' Severity remains Low.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a CoroutineExceptionHandler to each panel scope to surface unhandled exceptions in logs rather than swallowing them silently. No structural change required for lifecycle correctness — all dispose() paths are already wired.

confirmed.title: TagBuilderService @Service without platform-injected CoroutineScope takes Project but launches its own suspend work without a scope
confirmed.description: TagBuilderService is annotated `@Service(Service.Level.PROJECT)` but does not follow the platform-injected `cs: CoroutineScope` pattern mandated by the phase-4 conventions. Its `suspend` entry points (`scoreAndRankRuns`, `detectDockerTag`, `loadBaseline`) are called from caller-owned scopes (panel scopes), which is acceptable, but the IntelliJ DI constructor reads `PluginSettings` and `BuildLogCache` eagerly at construction time without deferring to lazy — in a headless test environment this will throw because `PluginSettings.getInstance(project)` invokes service resolution on the DI container before the plugin's state is initialised. The test constructor (the second one) is the workaround, but it means every test must remember to use the test constructor rather than `project.getService()`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Lazy-init `buildVariableName` by reading it at call time from `PluginSettings` rather than caching it at construction, so there is no settings read in the constructor. For `bambooService` and `buildLogCache`, the platform DI constructor already receives `Project` — use lazy delegates (`by lazy { project.getService(...) }`) consistent with the pattern used in `AutomationPanel` and `QueueService`. This removes the eager-resolution fragility without requiring a separate test constructor.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: TagBuilderService project constructor (line 29-35) reads PluginSettings.getInstance(project), project.getService(BambooService::class.java), and BuildLogCache.getInstance(project) eagerly at construction time. This is a convention gap relative to the platform-injected cs pattern and the lazy-delegate idiom used by QueueService. In headless tests, tests correctly use the second constructor (test constructor at lines 37-46) to avoid DI issues. The bambooService and buildVariableName fields are not lazy. In the test file, service = TagBuilderService(bambooService) is used directly. Practically the failure mode is contained by the test constructor seam. The finding is real as a maintainability/convention issue but not a runtime bug for production use since the DI context is always available when the platform constructs the service. Severity remains Low.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Apply lazy delegates for bambooService and buildLogCache; read buildVariableName at call time from PluginSettings to remove eager-construction fragility and align with the QueueService pattern. Remove the test constructor once lazy init is in place.

confirmed.title: QueueService.handleWaitingLocal: Bamboo-busy and getRunningBuilds-error paths untested
confirmed.description: Lines 391-392 contain two important branches with no direct tests: (a) `getRunningBuilds` returns an error (network/auth failure) — the entry must stay WAITING_LOCAL and not attempt a trigger; (b) `getRunningBuilds` returns a non-empty list (Bamboo has active builds) — the entry must stay WAITING_LOCAL. Neither scenario is covered. Without these tests, a regression that triggered builds when Bamboo is busy or when the connectivity check failed would not be caught.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add two tests in QueueServiceTest driving `pollOnce()` with a WAITING_LOCAL entry: (1) mock `getRunningBuilds` to return `ToolResult(isError=true)` and assert the entry stays WAITING_LOCAL; (2) mock `getRunningBuilds` to return a non-empty list (one running build key) and assert the entry stays WAITING_LOCAL and `triggerBuild` is never called (`coVerify(exactly=0) { bambooService.triggerBuild(any(), any(), any()) }`).
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: all three mock stubs for getRunningBuilds in QueueServiceTest (lines 567-570, 611-614, 700-703) return ToolResult.success(data = emptyList()). The two branches at QueueService.kt:392 — (a) runningResult.isError == true (network/auth failure, entry must stay WAITING_LOCAL), and (b) runningResult.data!!.isNotEmpty() (Bamboo has active builds, entry must stay WAITING_LOCAL) — are both untested. Given that handleWaitingLocal is the core auto-trigger decision path, a regression that triggers builds when Bamboo is busy or when connectivity fails would directly impact users in normal use. Severity is High as claimed.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add two tests to QueueServiceTest: one mocking getRunningBuilds to return isError=true and asserting the entry stays WAITING_LOCAL; one mocking it to return a non-empty list and asserting the entry stays WAITING_LOCAL with coVerify(exactly=0) { bambooService.triggerBuild(any(), any(), any()) }.

confirmed.title: QueueService.cancel(): cancelBuild invocation never verified for QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO entries
confirmed.description: Lines 213-215 show that `cancel()` calls `bambooService.cancelBuild(resultKey)` only when the entry is `QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO` and has a non-null `bambooResultKey`. The existing test only cancels a `WAITING_LOCAL` entry. There is no test that (a) seeds an entry already in `QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO` state with a `bambooResultKey` set, cancels it, and verifies `cancelBuild` is invoked; or (b) cancels a `WAITING_LOCAL` entry and verifies `cancelBuild` is NOT invoked.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that seeds a `QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO` entry with `bambooResultKey = "PROJ-AUTO-500"` directly in `_stateFlow` (or via `pollOnce` after a successful trigger), calls `service.cancel("q-1")`, and then uses `coVerify(exactly=1) { bambooService.cancelBuild("PROJ-AUTO-500") }`. Add a complementary test for a `WAITING_LOCAL` entry that verifies `coVerify(exactly=0) { bambooService.cancelBuild(any()) }`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: no test in QueueServiceTest exercises the cancelBuild path. The existing cancel test (lines 150-158) seeds a WAITING_LOCAL entry, never a QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO entry with a bambooResultKey set. The code path at QueueService.kt:212-215 — where cancelBuild(resultKey) is called only when entry.status == QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO and bambooResultKey != null — is unverified. A regression could silently drop the Bamboo-side cancel without any test catching it. High severity confirmed: cancelling a queued Bamboo build without actually calling cancelBuild would leave orphaned builds on the CI server.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test that seeds a QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO entry with bambooResultKey set, calls service.cancel(), and uses coVerify(exactly=1) { bambooService.cancelBuild('PROJ-AUTO-500') }. Add a complementary test for WAITING_LOCAL entry verifying coVerify(exactly=0) { bambooService.cancelBuild(any()) }.

confirmed.title: QueueService.restoreFromPersistence() has no test: crash-recovery path entirely uncovered
confirmed.description: Lines 501-511 implement `restoreFromPersistence()`, which reads active entries from SQLite and re-populates `_stateFlow`, then starts the poll loop if there is live work. This is the crash-recovery path invoked by `QueueRecoveryStartupActivity` on every IDE start. No test calls this method and verifies that (a) `stateFlow` is repopulated from the persistence layer, or (b) polling starts when non-terminal entries are found. A regression here silently orphans all pre-restart queue entries.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that pre-seeds the real `TagHistoryService` (with temp DB) with two `WAITING_LOCAL` entries via `saveQueueEntry`, constructs a `QueueService` with `autoTriggerEnabled=false`, calls `service.restoreFromPersistence()`, awaits the state via `awaitState { service.stateFlow.value.size == 2 }`, and asserts both entries are present with `WAITING_LOCAL` status. Also verify that an empty DB leaves `stateFlow` empty and no polling starts.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: grep across all test files finds only one mention of restoreFromPersistence in a comment (QueueServiceTest.kt:48). No test calls service.restoreFromPersistence() and verifies stateFlow is repopulated. The method at QueueService.kt:501-512 is the entire crash-recovery path invoked by QueueRecoveryStartupActivity on every IDE start. A regression silently orphaning all pre-restart queue entries would be invisible to the test suite. Severity is High as claimed — crash recovery is invoked on every IDE start and its correctness is entirely unverified.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test that pre-seeds TagHistoryService with WAITING_LOCAL entries via saveQueueEntry, constructs a QueueService, calls service.restoreFromPersistence(), awaits via awaitState, and asserts both the stateFlow size and that polling started. Also test the empty-DB case.

confirmed.title: QueueService.triggerNow() error path uncovered — failure result forwarding untested
confirmed.description: Lines 465-498 show `doTrigger` (called by `triggerNow`) returns a forwarded `ToolResult` with `isError=true` and propagated `payload` when `bambooService.triggerBuild` fails. Only the success path is tested (the single `triggerNow bypasses queue and triggers immediately` test mocks a successful response). The failure path — including that `isError=true`, that `payload` (ErrorType) is forwarded correctly, and that no state mutation occurs — is not tested.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that stubs `bambooService.triggerBuild` to return an error result with `ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED` payload, calls `service.triggerNow(entry)`, asserts `result.isError == true`, `result.data == ""`, and that the payload is `ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED`. Also verify that `_stateFlow.value` is unchanged (no entry mutation when triggerNow is called stand-alone).
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: QueueServiceTest line 217-232 has exactly one triggerNow test, which mocks a successful triggerBuild response and asserts assertFalse(result.isError). The error return path in doTrigger (QueueService.kt:492-498) — where isError=true and payload are forwarded from the bambooService.triggerBuild result — is untested via triggerNow. Note that the failure path through pollOnce/handleWaitingLocal is partially covered by the FAILED_TO_TRIGGER test (lines 605-646), but that goes through handleWaitingLocal, not triggerNow directly. The triggerNow error-return contract (that result.isError is propagated correctly, payload is forwarded, and no stateFlow mutation occurs) is not verified. Severity is Medium as the happy path is covered and the error path is partially exercised via the poll loop.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test that stubs bambooService.triggerBuild to return isError=true with ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED payload, calls service.triggerNow(entry), asserts result.isError == true, result.data == empty string, and payload == ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED.

confirmed.title: TagHistoryService.updateQueueEntryStatus errorMessage round-trip not tested
confirmed.description: Lines 199-232 show `updateQueueEntryStatus` accepts an `errorMessage: String?` parameter and writes it to the `error_message` column. The only existing test (line 111 in TagHistoryServiceTest) calls this method with `bambooResultKey` set but no `errorMessage`, and only checks `status` and `bambooResultKey`. The path where `errorMessage` is non-null (the `FAILED_TO_TRIGGER` flow writes `triggerResult.summary` as the errorMessage) is never round-tripped through SQLite.
confirmed.evidence:
UPDATE queue_entries SET status = ?, updated_at = ?, error_message = ?
WHERE id = ?
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that saves a `WAITING_LOCAL` entry, calls `service.updateQueueEntryStatus("q-1", QueueEntryStatus.FAILED_TO_TRIGGER, errorMessage = "Bamboo 401: token expired")`, then reads the DB via `getActiveQueueEntries()` — but since FAILED_TO_TRIGGER is terminal, probe SQLite directly (as the `dismiss` test does) and assert the `error_message` column equals `"Bamboo 401: token expired"`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed: TagHistoryServiceTest has only one updateQueueEntryStatus test at line 111-126, which calls service.updateQueueEntryStatus('q-1', QueueEntryStatus.RUNNING, bambooResultKey = 'PROJ-AUTO-849') with no errorMessage argument, and only checks status and bambooResultKey. The code at TagHistoryService.kt:199-232 has two SQL UPDATE branches: one with bambooResultKey (lines 208-218) and one without (lines 220-229). Both write errorMessage. The FAILED_TO_TRIGGER flow in QueueService writes errorMessage = triggerResult.summary (line 416-418). Neither branch's errorMessage write is covered by a round-trip SQLite test. Severity Medium is appropriate — this is a gap in the persistence contract test coverage but the code path is indirectly exercised by the FAILED_TO_TRIGGER test in QueueServiceTest (which checks the in-memory entry's errorMessage but not the SQLite column).
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a TagHistoryServiceTest that saves a WAITING_LOCAL entry, calls updateQueueEntryStatus with errorMessage = 'Bamboo 401: token expired', then probes the SQLite row directly via JDBC and asserts the error_message column equals the expected string.

confirmed.title: MonitorPanel.buildRunEntry: bambooUrl construction and filter buckets for most statuses untested
confirmed.description: Lines 800-870 implement `buildRunEntry`, which maps every `QueueEntryStatus` to a `RunEntry` with a computed `bambooUrl`, `filterBucket`, `isTerminal`, and `status` label. Only `FAILED_TO_TRIGGER` and `WAITING_LOCAL` are tested. The following are untested: (a) `bambooUrl = "$bambooUrlBase/browse/$resultKey"` when both are non-blank (line 801-803); (b) `COMPLETED` → `filterBucket=MonitorFilter.COMPLETED`, `isTerminal=true`; (c) `FAILED` → `filterBucket=FAILED`, `isTerminal=true`, `errorMessage` forwarded; (d) `CANCELLED` → `filterBucket=FAILED` (not CANCELLED); (e) `RUNNING` and `QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO` → not terminal.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Extend `MonitorPanelRunEntryTest` with parametrized or individual tests for each remaining status, using `bambooUrlBase = "https://bamboo.example.com"` and a non-blank `bambooResultKey`. Assert `bambooUrl`, `filterBucket`, `isTerminal`, and `status` label for each. In particular, assert that `CANCELLED` maps to `MonitorFilter.FAILED` (not a `CANCELLED` bucket) and that `COMPLETED` maps to `MonitorFilter.COMPLETED`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. MonitorPanelRunEntryTest.kt contains only 2 tests: one for FAILED_TO_TRIGGER and one for WAITING_LOCAL (lines 38-63). The source in MonitorPanel.kt lines 808-872 implements distinct logic for COMPLETED (filterBucket=COMPLETED, isTerminal=true), FAILED (filterBucket=FAILED, isTerminal=true, errorMessage forwarded), CANCELLED (filterBucket=FAILED — not a CANCELLED bucket), RUNNING (isTerminal=false, filterBucket=QUEUED), and QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO (isTerminal=false). None of these paths are exercised by any test. The bambooUrl construction at lines 801-803 (non-blank resultKey + non-blank bambooUrlBase) is also untested. However, buildRunEntry is a pure deterministic mapping function with no external dependencies — a regression here would be immediately visible in the UI, and the logic itself is straightforward. Severity is Medium as claimed, but lowered to Low because the function is pure, the logic is not complex, and there is no indication of a latent bug; this is purely a coverage gap.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add parametrized tests in MonitorPanelRunEntryTest covering each remaining QueueEntryStatus branch, including the bambooUrl construction with non-blank resultKey and bambooUrlBase, and asserting that CANCELLED maps to MonitorFilter.FAILED.

confirmed.title: QueueService.handleWaitingLocal: second-in-queue entry is not skipped (ordering contract untested)
confirmed.description: Lines 383-387 implement the oldest-entry-first ordering: only the first `WAITING_LOCAL` entry for a given `suitePlanKey` may trigger; subsequent entries must be skipped (`if (oldestWaiting?.id != entry.id) return entry`). No test verifies this contract. A regression that removes this guard would cause all WAITING_LOCAL entries for the same suite to fire simultaneously against Bamboo.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that enqueues two entries for the same `suitePlanKey` (q-1, q-2), mocks `getRunningBuilds` to return empty, mocks `triggerBuild` to succeed, calls `testService.pollOnce()`, and then asserts: only q-1 transitioned to `QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO`; q-2 remains `WAITING_LOCAL`; `coVerify(exactly=1) { bambooService.triggerBuild(any(), any(), any()) }`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. QueueService.kt lines 383-387 implement the oldest-entry-first guard: only the entry whose id matches the firstOrNull WAITING_LOCAL entry for the same suitePlanKey may proceed to trigger. Removal of this guard would cause concurrent triggering for all queued entries of the same suite. Reviewing QueueServiceTest.kt, the tests that exercise pollOnce() (e.g. 'transient triggerBuild error', 'FAILED_TO_TRIGGER preserves bamboo failure summary') all use single-entry setups. The 'getQueuePositionForSuite returns correct position' test does enqueue two entries for the same planKey but never calls pollOnce() — it only checks queue position metadata. No test enqueues two WAITING_LOCAL entries for the same suitePlanKey and verifies that only the first one triggers. The guard at line 387 (if (oldestWaiting?.id != entry.id) return entry) is load-bearing: removing it would allow all WAITING_LOCAL entries to fire Bamboo triggers simultaneously. Severity Medium is accurate.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test that enqueues two entries for the same suitePlanKey, mocks getRunningBuilds to return empty and triggerBuild to succeed, calls testService.pollOnce(), and asserts only q-1 transitions to QUEUED_ON_BAMBOO while q-2 remains WAITING_LOCAL, with coVerify(exactly=1) on triggerBuild.

confirmed.title: TagBuilderService.replaceCurrentRepoTag with null featureBranchTag returns entries unchanged — not tested
confirmed.description: Line 261 has an early-return guard: `if (context.featureBranchTag == null) return entries`. This path (the service could not detect a feature branch tag for the current repo) returns the baseline list untouched. It is not covered by any test in `TagBuilderServiceTest`. If the guard is accidentally removed or negated, every baseline entry for the current repo service would be overwritten with `null`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a unit test: construct a `CurrentRepoContext` with `featureBranchTag = null`, call `service.replaceCurrentRepoTag(entries, context)`, and assert the returned list is identical to the input list (`assertSame` or deep-equality) with no entry having `isCurrentRepo = true`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. TagBuilderService.kt line 261 has the early-return guard 'if (context.featureBranchTag == null) return entries'. TagBuilderServiceTest.kt has two tests for replaceCurrentRepoTag: one with a matching service and non-null featureBranchTag (line 77), and one with a non-matching service name (line 101). Neither test constructs a CurrentRepoContext with featureBranchTag=null. The null-tag path is a distinct code branch and is not covered. However, the risk is low because: the function is pure with no side effects, a null featureBranchTag simply means tag detection failed (legitimate scenario), and the guard is a simple early-return. Severity is correctly Low.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a unit test constructing CurrentRepoContext with featureBranchTag=null, calling replaceCurrentRepoTag, and asserting the returned list is referentially or deeply equal to the input with no entry having isCurrentRepo=true.

confirmed.title: BaselineCacheService.invalidate for non-existent key (no-op early return) untested
confirmed.description: Line 89 has an early return: `if (entries.remove(planKey) == null) return`. This `return` exits the suspend function before calling `persistSnapshot`, so no disk write occurs when the key did not exist. The scenario is not tested. If this guard is inadvertently removed (e.g. by collapsing the null check), a spurious file-write — overwriting the cache with a missing key still included — would occur on every call to `invalidate` for an unknown key.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that calls `svc.invalidate("NON-EXISTENT")` on a freshly constructed empty cache, then asserts `svc.get("NON-EXISTENT") == null` (no crash) and that no `baseline-cache.json` file was created in the cache directory (i.e. `!cacheFile.exists()`), confirming the no-op early return fires correctly.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. BaselineCacheService.kt lines 87-92 implement the invalidate() function with an early return when entries.remove(planKey) == null (key not present). The existing test at BaselineCacheServiceTest.kt line 87-92 ('invalidate removes the entry') only tests the happy path where the key exists. No test calls invalidate() on a non-existent key. If the null check is removed, invalidate() would call persistSnapshot() with an unchanged map snapshot on every no-op call — a spurious but harmless disk write. The risk of regression is very low because the guard only prevents unnecessary disk I/O. Severity is correctly Low.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test calling svc.invalidate("NON-EXISTENT") on a freshly constructed empty cache, asserting no crash and that no baseline-cache.json file was written to the temp directory.

confirmed.title: TagBuilderService.scoreAndRankRuns: require() precondition violations (maxWalk < targetParseable, non-positive targetParseable) untested
confirmed.description: Lines 81-82 have two `require()` guards: `require(targetParseable > 0)` and `require(maxWalk >= targetParseable)`. No test exercises these guards. A caller passing `targetParseable=0` or `maxWalk < targetParseable` would throw an `IllegalArgumentException` at runtime without any test evidence that the contract is enforced. Callers currently always use defaults, but future call sites (e.g. a configurable baseline depth setting) could violate these preconditions silently.
confirmed.evidence:
require(targetParseable > 0) { "targetParseable must be positive" }
require(maxWalk >= targetParseable) { "maxWalk ($maxWalk) must be >= targetParseable ($targetParseable)" }
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add two tests using `assertThrows<IllegalArgumentException>` wrapped in `runTest`: one calling `service.scoreAndRankRuns("PROJ", targetParseable = 0)` and asserting the exception message contains "positive"; another calling `service.scoreAndRankRuns("PROJ", targetParseable = 5, maxWalk = 3)` and asserting the exception message contains "maxWalk".
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. TagBuilderService.kt lines 81-82 have two require() guards: require(targetParseable > 0) and require(maxWalk >= targetParseable). TagBuilderServiceTest.kt has no test that exercises either guard with a violating argument; all existing tests use valid parameter values (defaulting to targetParseable=10, maxWalk=10, or using explicit valid values like targetParseable=6, maxWalk=12 and targetParseable=3, maxWalk=10). The guards are already enforced at runtime by Kotlin's require() — violation throws IllegalArgumentException immediately. No latent bug is present; this is purely a coverage gap. Future call sites with user-configurable values (e.g. a settings field for baseline depth) could hit these guards unexpectedly. Severity Low is accurate since all current callers pass defaults or otherwise valid values, and the precondition is already enforced by the language construct.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add two tests using assertThrows<IllegalArgumentException> in runTest: one for targetParseable=0 asserting the message contains 'positive', and one for targetParseable=5, maxWalk=3 asserting the message contains 'maxWalk'.

confirmed.title: UI mutations on IO thread: onRescan() called from Dispatchers.IO after applyFixes
confirmed.description: `onFixAll()` launches a coroutine on `Dispatchers.IO` (line 154: `scope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)`). After the `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block (lines 156–169), execution returns to the IO thread and line 171 calls `onRescan()`. Inside `onRescan()`, lines 113–114 directly mutate Swing components: `rescanButton.isEnabled = false` and `statusLabel.text = "Scanning..."`. These UI mutations execute on the IO thread, violating IntelliJ's threading model and risking visual corruption or JVM-level assertion failures under the Event Dispatch Thread checker.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Wrap the `onRescan()` call inside a `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block at line 171, or change `onRescan()` itself to not mutate UI state directly when called from a non-EDT context. Specifically: replace line 171 with `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { onRescan() }`. Alternatively refactor so the post-fix rescan always starts via `SwingUtilities.invokeLater { onRescan() }`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. In `onFixAll()` (line 154), the outer `scope.launch {}` runs on `Dispatchers.IO` (the scope is declared at line 55 as `CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)`). The `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block (lines 156–169) correctly bounces UI mutations to EDT, but on exit it returns to the IO dispatcher. Line 171 then calls `onRescan()` directly on the IO thread. Inside `onRescan()`, lines 113–114 perform `rescanButton.isEnabled = false` and `statusLabel.text = "Scanning..."` as synchronous Swing mutations without any EDT dispatch guard. These are off-EDT Swing mutations, which violate IntelliJ's threading model and can trigger `IllegalStateException` from the EDT checker, or silently corrupt component state. The fix (wrapping the `onRescan()` call in `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)`) does not break any passing tests and is straightforwardly correct — `onRescan()` itself immediately launches a new IO coroutine and marshals all subsequent UI updates back to EDT.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Wrap the call at line 171 with `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { onRescan() }` so the initial Swing mutations in `onRescan()` lines 113–114 execute on the EDT.

confirmed.title: Copyright fix silently no-ops for headers between lines 15–30 (scan vs fix window mismatch)
confirmed.description: `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile` scans the first **30** lines to detect `YEAR_OUTDATED` (lines 111, 141 in `CopyrightFixService.kt`). However, `applyFixes` in `CopyrightFixCard` reads only the first **15** lines as `headerRegion` (line 221) before calling `updateYearInHeader`. For files where the copyright year appears between lines 16–30 (common for Apache/EUPL/GPL headers), `analyzeFile` correctly classifies them `YEAR_OUTDATED`, but `applyFixes` misses the year in the 15-line window, `updateYearInHeader` returns the region unchanged, and no document mutation is written. The UI reports "Fixed N file(s)" but the file is untouched — a silent correctness failure.
confirmed.evidence:
CopyrightFixService.kt:141:        val headerRegion = content.lines().take(30).joinToString("\n")
CopyrightFixCard.kt:221:                            val headerRegion = original.lines().take(15).joinToString("\n")
confirmed.suggestedFix: Change `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes` line 221 from `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` to match the scan window used in `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile`. Also update the corresponding line 225 `drop(15)` to `drop(30)`. Extract the magic constant `30` into a shared `const val HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30` in `CopyrightFixService` and reference it from both the service and the card.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile` (line 141) and `hasCopyrightHeader` (line 111) both scan the first 30 lines to classify files as `YEAR_OUTDATED`. `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes` (line 221) reads only `original.lines().take(15)` and reassembles with `original.lines().drop(15)` (line 226). For any file where the copyright year appears on lines 16–30 (common for Apache/EUPL/GPL/LGPL preambles), `analyzeFile` correctly returns `YEAR_OUTDATED`, but `applyFixes` passes a 15-line window to `updateYearInHeader`, which finds no year and returns the region unchanged. The document is not mutated, yet `statusLabel.text = "Fixed N file(s)"` is shown — a silent correctness failure. Verified by reading both files; the constant divergence is unambiguous at those exact lines. The fix (changing take(15)/drop(15) to take(30)/drop(30)) carries zero risk of breaking passing tests since existing `CopyrightFixServiceTest` tests operate on single-line or short headers entirely within the 15-line window.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Change `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` at line 221 and `original.lines().drop(15)` to `original.lines().drop(30)` at line 226 in `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes`. Extract the constant 30 into `CopyrightFixService.HEADER_SCAN_LINES` and reference it from both files.

confirmed.title: Non-atomic read-modify-write on HandoverStateService._stateFlow lost under concurrent events and EDT mark calls
confirmed.description: `handleEvent` reads `_stateFlow.value` into `current` (line 220), computes `next`, then writes `_stateFlow.value = next` (line 326). Concurrently, `markCopyrightFixed()` (331), `markJiraCommentPosted()` (336), `markJiraTransitioned()` (341–343), and `markWorkLogged()` (349) all do the same non-atomic pattern from the EDT. Since `handleEvent` runs on the coroutine collector thread and the `markX()` methods are called from EDT, concurrent invocations can overwrite each other. Specifically: if `handleEvent` reads `current` (with `copyrightFixed=true`), then `markCopyrightFixed()` writes `copyrightFixed=true` first, then `handleEvent` overwrites with `next` whose `copyrightFixed` reflects the value at the time of the earlier read — the `markCopyrightFixed()` write is silently clobbered, causing the checklist dot to revert to PENDING incorrectly. Note: `resetStatusSlices()` correctly uses `_stateFlow.update { }` (line 113) but the other methods do not.
confirmed.evidence:
220:        val current = _stateFlow.value
326:        _stateFlow.value = next
---
331:        _stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(copyrightFixed = true)
349:        _stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(todayWorkLogged = true)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace all non-atomic read-modify-write patterns with `_stateFlow.update { current -> current.copy(...) }`. In `handleEvent`, change: `val current = _stateFlow.value; val next = when(event) { ... }; _stateFlow.value = next` to `_stateFlow.update { current -> when(event) { ... } }`. Similarly change all `markX()` methods to use `_stateFlow.update { it.copy(field = value) }`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real pattern, severity downgraded from High to Medium. The non-atomic read-modify-write is real: `handleEvent` (line 220 read, line 326 write) and all four `markX()` methods (lines 331, 336, 341–343, 349) use `_stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(...)` instead of `_stateFlow.update { }`. A race window exists between the IO-dispatched `handleEvent` coroutine and EDT-dispatched `markX()` calls. However, in practice, the user-actioned slices (`copyrightFixed`, `todayWorkLogged`, `jiraTransitioned`) are only set once per ticket lifecycle and are ticket-level (not PR-level), so the clobbering window requires improbably tight timing between a build/quality event and a manual user action. The harm if it does occur is a checklist dot reverting to PENDING until the next event. `resetStatusSlices()` correctly uses `update {}` (line 113), confirming the author was aware of the atomic API. Applying the fix cannot break any existing test since all `markX` tests run sequentially with Dispatchers.Unconfined.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Replace all non-atomic `_stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(...)` patterns in `markX()` methods and the `handleEvent` tail-write with `_stateFlow.update { current -> current.copy(...) }` to match the correct pattern already used in `resetStatusSlices()`.

confirmed.title: JiraPreviewPane liveResults collector job is leaked — never cancelled on Disposable.dispose()
confirmed.description: `JiraPreviewPane.init` creates an isolated root `Job` (`val collectorJob = Job()`, line 90) and launches a `SharedFlow.collect` coroutine on `CoroutineScope(cs.coroutineContext + collectorJob)`. Because `collectorJob` has no parent (it is not a child of `cs`'s Job), it is never cancelled when either `cs` is cancelled or when `dispose()` is called (line 131). Every `JiraPreviewPane` instance that is constructed but later replaced (e.g., when a template is selected, triggering a preview pane re-construction, or when the tool window tab is closed and re-opened) leaves a permanently-running SharedFlow collector that keeps the pane object reachable and continues processing `liveResults` events for stale content.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Store `collectorJob` as a field and cancel it in `dispose()`: `private val collectorJob = Job()` at class level, use it in `init` as before, and add `collectorJob.cancel()` to the `dispose()` method. Alternatively — since the intent is to avoid the collector being a child of `cs` — use `cs.launch` with a `Job()` stored as a field and cancelled on dispose.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. `collectorJob = Job()` at line 90 is a local variable scoped to the `init` block; it is never stored as a field and never cancelled in `dispose()` (lines 131–133). The comment at line 88 correctly explains the intent (avoid UncompletedCoroutinesError in runTest), but the consequence is that every `JiraPreviewPane` instance leaves a permanently-running `SharedFlow.collect` coroutine when the pane is replaced or the tool window closes. The `dispose()` body is a no-op. This is a resource leak: the stale pane remains reachable through the running coroutine and processes `liveResults` events forever. The fix (store `collectorJob` as a field and call `collectorJob.cancel()` in `dispose()`) is safe — `JiraPreviewPaneTest` constructs panes via `newPane(scope)` inside `runTest`, and since the test scope is cancelled at the end of runTest, the collectorJob (which uses `cs.coroutineContext`) would also be cancelled. Existing tests pass with or without the fix.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Promote `collectorJob` to a private field (`private val collectorJob = Job()`), use it in `init` as already written, and add `collectorJob.cancel()` to the `dispose()` method.

confirmed.title: handleCopyWiki emits COPY_EMAIL action instead of a wiki-specific action, corrupting override audit log
confirmed.description: `handleCopyWiki()` (line 159–166) emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL` at line 160 when the user copies the Jira wiki-format text to clipboard. The correct semantic is that the user performed a "Copy wiki" action (distinct from copying formatted HTML email). `HandoverOverrideTracker` persists every `HandoverOverride` event with its action type; since `COPY_WIKI` does not exist in the `HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`), the developer picked the wrong value. This means the override audit log misidentifies wiki copies as email copies, making the 30-day count in Settings unreliable for distinguishing which handover paths the user actually used.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in `WorkflowEvent.kt` (next to `COPY_EMAIL`). Then change line 160 in `ShareTab.kt` to `emitOverrideIfNeeded(WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI)`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real but severity downgraded from Medium to Low. `handleCopyWiki()` line 160 emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL` instead of a wiki-specific action. The `HandoverAction` enum (WorkflowEvent.kt:90) has no `COPY_WIKI` value. The override audit log therefore misidentifies wiki copies as email copies. However, the impact is confined to the 30-day override counter in Settings — it does not affect any functional behavior (the clipboard operation itself is correct), no data is lost, and users are unlikely to inspect the override log breakdown by action type. The fix (adding `COPY_WIKI` to the enum and updating line 160) is an additive, non-breaking change. It cannot break any passing tests; the `ShareTabTest` does not assert on the emitted action type.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in `WorkflowEvent.kt` and change `ShareTab.handleCopyWiki()` line 160 to emit `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI`.

confirmed.title: HandoverAiSummaryCache.getOrCompute uses coroutineScope{} that blocks the caller until the LLM response arrives, defeating Deferred coalescing intent
confirmed.description: `getOrCompute` wraps `cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { async { ... } }` inside `coroutineScope { }` (lines 165–182). `coroutineScope { }` suspends until **all its child coroutines** complete. Since the `async { ... }` block (the LLM call) is launched as a child of this `coroutineScope`, the scope does not return until the LLM call finishes. The returned `Deferred` is already completed at line 183 when `deferred.await()` is called. The class documentation says the Deferred coalescing allows concurrent callers to "share one in-flight compute" without blocking until `await()`, but because each caller's `coroutineScope { }` blocks until the LLM response arrives, the concurrency benefit is lost — every caller to `changeSummary()` / `ticketSummary()` will block at the `coroutineScope { }` boundary for the full LLM latency, even on a cache hit for the second-concurrent caller.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace the `coroutineScope { }` wrapper with the service's own `cs` scope: `val deferred = cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { cs.async { ... } }`. This makes `async { }` a child of the persistent service scope rather than a transient `coroutineScope`, preserving the Deferred in the map across coroutine cancellations of individual callers. The existing `deferred.await()` + `cache.remove(key, deferred)` in the catch block (lines 183–191) then correctly handles cancellation cleanup.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. The `coroutineScope { }` at line 165 launches `async { LLM call }` as its child coroutine. `coroutineScope` suspends until ALL its children complete, so the first caller's `coroutineScope {}` block does not return until the LLM call finishes — the returned `Deferred` is already completed at line 182. The `deferred.await()` at line 183 then returns immediately with the cached result. For truly concurrent callers, a second caller's `computeIfAbsent` lambda does not fire (existing Deferred returned), so its `coroutineScope {}` has no children and exits immediately, then `deferred.await()` correctly suspends on the in-progress Deferred from the first caller. Coalescing works in the sense that only one LLM call is made, but the documentation claim that "concurrent callers share one in-flight compute" without blocking is violated for the first caller, who always blocks the full LLM latency inside `coroutineScope {}` before `getOrCompute` returns. The fix (using `cs.async {}` directly without `coroutineScope {}`) correctly makes `getOrCompute` return the in-flight Deferred immediately, allowing the caller to do other work and await later. Existing `HandoverAiSummaryCacheTest` tests pass either way since they use sequential `changeSummary()` calls.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Replace `coroutineScope { cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { async { ... } } }` with `cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { cs.async { ... } }` so the Deferred is parented to the service scope and `getOrCompute` returns the in-flight Deferred immediately without blocking the caller.

confirmed.title: HandoverState.currentBranch is a dead field — defined but never read
confirmed.description: The `currentBranch: String = ""` field on `HandoverState` (line 9) is declared but never written to (no `copy(currentBranch = ...)` call anywhere in the module) and never read by any consumer (`ChecksTab`, `HandoverPanel`, `ShareTab`, `HandoverTicketHeader`, `HandoverPlaceholderResolver` — none reference it). It is also never set during `resetForNewTicket` or `resetStatusSlices`. It is a dead data field carrying zero information.
confirmed.evidence: val currentBranch: String = "",  // line 9 — zero grep hits for reads in handover/src/main/
confirmed.suggestedFix: Delete `val currentBranch: String = ""` from `HandoverState`. If branch context is needed in future, source it from `WorkflowContextService.state.value.activeBranch` (the canonical source) rather than a stale mirror on `HandoverState`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real dead field. `HandoverState.currentBranch: String = ""` (HandoverModels.kt line 9) has exactly zero read references anywhere in the handover module's src/main tree. A grep across the full handover/src/main tree yields only the declaration site. It is never written via `copy(currentBranch = ...)` in `HandoverStateService`, never read in `ChecksTab`, `HandoverPanel`, `ShareTab`, `HandoverTicketHeader`, or `HandoverPlaceholderResolver`. It is not reset in `resetForNewTicket` or `resetStatusSlices`. Deleting it has no behavioral impact; it cannot break any passing test.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Delete `val currentBranch: String = ""` from `HandoverState`. Branch context should be sourced from `WorkflowContextService.state.value.activeBranch` if needed in the future.

confirmed.title: JiraClosureService.project field is stored but never consumed
confirmed.description: Lines 16-19 declare `private var project: Project? = null` and the DI constructor stores the `project` argument into it. Neither `buildClosureComment()` nor `mergeDockerTags()` — the only methods on the class — ever reference `this.project`. The field is write-only dead storage. The `@TestOnly` no-arg constructor leaves `project` null, confirming the service logic does not need it.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Remove the `private var project: Project? = null` field and the `this.project = project` assignment from the DI constructor. The `Project` parameter in `constructor(project: Project)` and `companion.getInstance(project)` are correct IntelliJ DI boilerplate and must remain; only the stored field is dead.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real dead field. `JiraClosureService` stores `private var project: Project? = null` (line 16) and writes to it in the DI constructor (line 19). Neither `buildClosureComment()` nor `mergeDockerTags()` — the only non-companion methods — reference `this.project` or `project`. The `@TestOnly` no-arg constructor leaves `project` null, which is consistent with the service logic not needing it. The `Project` parameter in `constructor(project: Project)` and `companion.getInstance(project)` are correct IntelliJ service DI boilerplate and must remain. Only the stored field and the assignment `this.project = project` are dead. Removing them has no behavioral impact.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Remove the `private var project: Project? = null` field declaration and the `this.project = project` assignment from the DI constructor. Keep the `constructor(project: Project)` signature and `companion.getInstance(project)` unchanged.

confirmed.title: TimeTrackingService.hoursToSeconds() is dead in production — test-only callers
confirmed.description: The public `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` (line 42) is called only from `TimeTrackingServiceTest` (lines 36-40). No production code in `:handover`, `:agent`, `:core`, or any other module calls it. `TimeLogCard.onLogClicked()` uses `hoursToJiraTimeString()` instead. The method was originally planned as a utility for `JiraService.logWork` but the design shifted to use the string form. A `docs/research/2026-05-07-handover-wireup-plan.md` note documents this transition.
confirmed.evidence:
fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int = (hours * 3600).toInt()  // line 42
// Grep across entire worktree: only callers are TimeTrackingServiceTest lines 36-40
confirmed.suggestedFix: Delete `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int = (hours * 3600).toInt()` from `TimeTrackingService` and delete the four test cases in `TimeTrackingServiceTest` that exercise it. No production callsite exists.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` at TimeTrackingService.kt:42 has exactly one caller: TimeTrackingServiceTest.kt lines 37-40. A worktree-wide grep finds no production callsite in any module. TimeLogCard.onLogClicked() (line 216) uses `hoursToJiraTimeString()` instead, which is the correct path per the Jira REST API requirement. Deleting the method and its four test assertions is safe — no production behavior changes.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Delete `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` from TimeTrackingService.kt and remove the `hoursToSeconds converts correctly` test in TimeTrackingServiceTest.kt.

confirmed.title: TimeTrackingService.formatStartedDate() and ISO_FORMAT are dead in production
confirmed.description: `fun formatStartedDate(year, month, day, hour, minute)` (lines 64-70) and the companion `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87) are called only from `TimeTrackingServiceTest.formatStartedDate produces ISO 8601` — no production code calls them. `TimeLogCard.onLogClicked()` (line 221) constructs the `OffsetDateTime` directly via `LocalDateTime.of(...).atOffset(ZoneOffset.UTC)` (a comment there even references the former `formatStartedDate(.., 9, 0)` call that was replaced). The three imports `LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter` become dead if this method is removed.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Delete `fun formatStartedDate(...)` (lines 64-70), `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87), and the three now-unused imports (`LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter`). Delete the corresponding test case `formatStartedDate produces ISO 8601`. Note: `JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856` has a code comment `"matches TimeTrackingService.ISO_FORMAT"` — update that comment to reference the local pattern string directly.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `fun formatStartedDate(...)` at lines 64-70 and `private val ISO_FORMAT` at line 87 have no production callers. TimeLogCard.kt:220-223 constructs the `OffsetDateTime` inline (the comment on line 220 explicitly notes this replaced the `formatStartedDate` call). The only test caller is TimeTrackingServiceTest:44-48. JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856 references `TimeTrackingService.ISO_FORMAT` only in a KDoc comment, not in executable code — updating that comment is needed but not harmful. Deleting the method removes `LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter` imports from TimeTrackingService as well, since they are unused after removal.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Delete `fun formatStartedDate(...)` (lines 64-70) and `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87) from TimeTrackingService.kt, remove the now-dead imports, delete the corresponding test, and update the KDoc in JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856 to reference the local pattern string.

confirmed.title: CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes uses take(15) but CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile uses take(30) — silent no-op for headers in lines 16-30
confirmed.description: In `applyFixes()` (line 221), the header region scanned for year replacement is `original.lines().take(15)`. However, `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile()` (lines 111, 141) was intentionally raised to `take(30)` to detect long Apache/EUPL headers (audit finding handover:F-3, confirmed by test `analyzeFile scans 30 lines for year update`). This mismatch means: `analyzeFile` correctly marks a file `YEAR_OUTDATED` when the copyright year appears on lines 16-30, but `applyFixes` only reads lines 1-15 and calls `service.updateYearInHeader(headerRegion)` on that truncated region — which finds nothing to update, falls through to `if (rewritten == headerRegion) original`, and silently leaves the file unchanged. The Fix All button reports success but the year is not updated.
confirmed.suggestedFix:
Change line 221 of `CopyrightFixCard.kt` from `val headerRegion = original.lines().take(15).joinToString("
")` to `val headerRegion = original.lines().take(30).joinToString("
")`. Also change line 227 from `val rest = original.lines().drop(15).joinToString("
")` to `drop(30)` to reassemble the full file correctly. Add a regression test in `CopyrightFixServiceTest` covering a year on line 20.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed mismatch. CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() at lines 111 and 141 both use `content.lines().take(30)` to detect and assess outdated years. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 reads `original.lines().take(15)` for the header region passed to `service.updateYearInHeader(...)`, then reassembles with `drop(15)` at line 226. For any file where the copyright year appears on lines 16-30, analyzeFile() correctly marks it YEAR_OUTDATED, but applyFixes() searches only lines 1-15, finds nothing to update, and the `if (rewritten == headerRegion) original` branch leaves the file unchanged. The UI reports success but the year is not updated — a silent no-op for long Apache/EUPL headers. The fix (change take/drop constants from 15 to 30) is surgical and cannot break correct behavior for files whose copyright is within the first 15 lines.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Change CopyrightFixCard.kt line 221 from `take(15)` to `take(30)`, and line 226 from `drop(15)` to `drop(30)`. Add a regression test covering a copyright year on line 20.

confirmed.title: HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.escape() duplicates core HtmlEscape.escapeHtml and is incomplete (misses single-quote)
confirmed.description: Lines 325-326 define a private `escape(s: String)` inside the `HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer` object that replaces `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`. The listed core utility `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml()` (in `core/src/main/kotlin/com/workflow/orchestrator/core/util/HtmlEscape.kt`) performs identical escaping but also handles `'` → `'`. The `:handover` module already depends on `:core` (`implementation(project(":core"))`), so `HtmlEscape` is directly importable. This private reinvention (a) is flagged by the audit rule, (b) silently omits the apostrophe — a template author's name or ticket title containing `'` would render unescaped in the preview HTML.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Delete the private `escape(s: String)` function and replace the three call sites (`escape(it)` in `applyInline`, `escape(it)` in the code block, `escape(it)` in the heading handler) with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml(it)`, adding `import com.workflow.orchestrator.core.util.HtmlEscape`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.escape() at lines 325-326 replaces `&`, `<`, `>`, `"` but omits `'` → `'`. HtmlEscape.escapeHtml() in core includes the apostrophe replacement and is importable from :handover (which already depends on :core). The missing apostrophe escape is a real gap: a template author name like `O'Brien` in a heading or table cell would render with a literal `'` in the HTML, which is technically valid in HTML5 body text but inconsistent with the core utility. The fix — replace the private `escape()` calls with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml()` — cannot break correct behavior since the core method is a superset.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Delete the private `escape(s: String)` function and replace its three call sites in `applyInline` and the code-block handler with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml(it)`, adding the appropriate import.

confirmed.title: handlePostComment and handleCopyFormatted carry a dead @Suppress(UNUSED_PARAMETER) template param
confirmed.description: Both `handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template)` (line 141) and `handleCopyFormatted(resolvedMarkup, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template)` (line 168) accept a `HandoverTemplate` second parameter they never use. The `@Suppress` is self-documenting dead weight. The template context is already fully applied before these methods are called (the caller resolves markup via `placeholderResolver`, so passing the raw template adds nothing). These are internal methods wired only from the `create()` lambdas at lines 280 and 294.
confirmed.evidence:
internal suspend fun handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate) {  // line 141
internal suspend fun handleCopyFormatted(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate) {  // line 168
confirmed.suggestedFix: Remove the `template: HandoverTemplate` parameter from `handlePostComment` and `handleCopyFormatted`. Update their lambda wrappers at lines 280 and 294 from `{ markup, tmpl -> holder[0]?.handlePostComment(markup, tmpl) }` to `{ markup, _ -> holder[0]?.handlePostComment(markup) }`. Also update the `@TestOnly` methods `testTriggerPostComment` and `testTriggerCopyFormatted` (lines 224, 229) accordingly.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. ShareTab.kt line 141: `handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate)` — the `template` parameter is never read inside the method body. Same at line 168 for `handleCopyFormatted`. The `@Suppress` annotation itself acknowledges the parameter is dead. The callers at the lambda wiring (lines 280 and 294) pass the template only to satisfy the signature. Removing the parameter requires updating the lambda signatures at those two call sites and the two `@TestOnly` methods at lines 224 and 229. This is a mechanical refactor with no behavioral change.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Remove `template: HandoverTemplate` parameter from `handlePostComment` and `handleCopyFormatted`, update the lambda wrappers at lines 280/294, and update `testTriggerPostComment`/`testTriggerCopyFormatted` accordingly.

confirmed.title: handleCopyWiki emits COPY_EMAIL override action — wrong telemetry for wiki-clipboard action
confirmed.description: Line 160 of `handleCopyWiki` calls `emitOverrideIfNeeded(WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL)`. This is the wiki-markup-copy path (copies Jira wiki text to clipboard), not the HTML email path. Both `handleCopyWiki` (line 160) and `handleCopyFormatted` (line 169) emit `COPY_EMAIL`, making them indistinguishable in the `HandoverOverrideTracker` audit log and in `HandoverConfigurable`'s 30-day count. The `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`) has no `COPY_WIKI` variant, so the wiki-copy action is silently mislabeled.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add `COPY_WIKI` to `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction` in `core/src/main/kotlin/com/workflow/orchestrator/core/events/WorkflowEvent.kt`. Update `handleCopyWiki` (line 160) to emit `HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI` instead of `COPY_EMAIL`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. ShareTab.kt line 160: `handleCopyWiki` emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL`, which is semantically wrong for a wiki-markup clipboard action. However, examining HandoverOverrideTracker.kt, the tracker records only timestamps (not action types) in `handoverOverrideLog` — so the audit log does not distinguish action types at all today. The `HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`) has no `COPY_WIKI` variant. The practical impact is limited to the `WorkflowEvent.HandoverOverride` payload being mislabeled; no downstream code routes on `COPY_EMAIL` vs. a hypothetical `COPY_WIKI`. The severity is Low, not the claimed Low — the finding is real but the actual observable impact in the current codebase is nil since the tracker ignores the action value.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in WorkflowEvent.kt, then update `handleCopyWiki` at line 160 to emit `HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI`.

confirmed.title: Stale KDoc comment in HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer — T10 was already delivered
confirmed.description: Line 176 says `"T10 will wrap this with a project-scoped service that adds a live-render path."` but T10 is fully delivered — `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService` (the wrapping service) is defined in the same file (lines 29-167) immediately above the `HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer` object. The comment implies future work that is already done, which is misleading for maintainers.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace the KDoc line with: `"Wrapped by [HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService] (above) which adds a live Jira render path."` or remove the sentence.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.kt line 176 reads `"T10 will wrap this with a project-scoped service that adds a live-render path."` but `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService` is already fully defined in the same file at lines 29-167. The KDoc is misleading about future work that was already delivered. Fixing it is a trivial one-line KDoc update with zero behavioral impact.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Replace the stale KDoc sentence with `"Wrapped by [HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService] (above) which provides the live Jira render path."` or remove it entirely.

confirmed.title: TimeLogCard.setTicket() has zero call sites — Log Work permanently disabled
confirmed.description: TimeLogCard declares a public `setTicket(ticketKey: String?)` method at line 140, but it is never called anywhere in the codebase. The field `activeTicketId` initializes to `""` and is never updated in production. `refreshLogButtonState()` checks `ticketOk = activeTicketId.isNotBlank()` which is always false, so the Log Work button is permanently disabled and the card always shows the empty state 'Select a ticket to log time.' HandoverPanel.applyState() (line 101) fans state only to `header.updateState(state)`, `checksTab.updateState(state)`, and `banner.setFailures(...)` — ActionsTab/TimeLogCard receive nothing. The 'todayWorkLogged' checklist dot can never turn green via the UI card.
confirmed.suggestedFix: In HandoverPanel.applyState() (or by subscribing TimeLogCard to WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow), call `timeLog.setTicket(state.ticketId.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() })` after each state emission. Since HandoverPanel does not hold a reference to the inner TimeLogCard, either (a) expose a method on ActionsTab that delegates to TimeLogCard, or (b) wire ActionsTab to collect `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` directly in its own scope and call timeLog.setTicket() on changes.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. A full worktree grep finds `setTicket` defined at TimeLogCard.kt:140 with zero production callers. ActionsTab (the container) does not call it. HandoverPanel.applyState() (line 101-112) fans state only to `header.updateState(state)`, `checksTab.updateState(state)`, and `banner.setFailures(...)` — ActionsTab and TimeLogCard receive no ticket-change notifications. TimeLogCard does not subscribe to `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` internally; it relies entirely on `setTicket()` being called externally. Since `activeTicketId` is initialized to `""` (line 71) and never updated, `refreshLogButtonState()` always computes `ticketOk = false`, keeping the Log Work button permanently disabled. The card always shows the empty state label. The CLAUDE.md for :handover explicitly states TimeLogCard calls `JiraService.logWork` and flips `markWorkLogged()`, but that code path is unreachable via the UI because the precondition for enabling the button is never satisfied. The fix — having ActionsTab or TimeLogCard subscribe to `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` — is load-bearing functionality restoration, not harmful.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Wire `TimeLogCard.setTicket()` by subscribing to `WorkflowContextService.getInstance(project).activeTicketFlow` inside `TimeLogCard.init` (preferred, self-contained) or by exposing a delegation method on `ActionsTab` and calling it from `HandoverPanel.applyState()`. The self-contained subscription in TimeLogCard is architecturally cleaner since the card already holds the project reference.

confirmed.title: CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes reads only 15 header lines while CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile scans 30
confirmed.description: CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() (CopyrightFixService.kt line 111) scans the first 30 lines for copyright headers. When it finds a YEAR_OUTDATED entry from lines 16–30, it stores the stale year expression. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 then reads only `original.lines().take(15)` to build its `headerRegion`. updateYearInHeader() finds no match in the 15-line region, so `rewritten == headerRegion` and the file is left unchanged. The UI reports a failed fix attempt but the status stays YEAR_OUTDATED indefinitely. The scan-window mismatch is the root cause.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Change line 221 in CopyrightFixCard.kt from `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` to match the 30-line window used by CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile(). Also update the corresponding reassembly at line 224: change `.drop(15)` to `.drop(30)`. Consider extracting the constant (e.g., `HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30`) into CopyrightFixService.companion so both paths share it.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() at line 111 uses content.lines().take(30) to detect and classify a YEAR_OUTDATED header. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 uses original.lines().take(15) as the headerRegion passed to updateYearInHeader(). When the year expression falls on lines 16–30, updateYearInHeader() finds no match in the 15-line region, returns the unchanged string, the rewritten == headerRegion guard at line 223 triggers, and the file is left unmodified. The fix (changing take(15)/drop(15) to take(30)/drop(30)) is correct and harmless. The CopyrightCellRenderer already imports JBLabel (line 13) so there are no import conflicts. Note the suggested constant extraction is optional but the core 15→30 change is the fix.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Change CopyrightFixCard.kt line 221 from original.lines().take(15) to original.lines().take(30), and line 226 (the drop) from drop(15) to drop(30). Optionally extract a HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30 constant into CopyrightFixService.companion.

confirmed.title: WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted has no production emit site — EventBus handler in HandoverStateService is dead code
confirmed.description: HandoverStateService.handleEvent() at lines 319–321 handles WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted and flips jiraCommentPosted=true. However, no production code ever emits this event: JiraServiceImpl.addComment() (jira module) returns a ToolResult but does not call eventBus.emit(JiraCommentPosted(...)). A global search confirms the only emit sites are in tests. The 'Jira comment posted' checklist dot can only turn green via the direct markJiraCommentPosted() call in ShareTab.handlePostComment(). If a comment is posted via the agent's jira tool (JiraTool action add_comment), the HandoverState is never updated.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add an EventBus emission in JiraServiceImpl.addComment() on success: `eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(key, commentId))`. Since JiraServiceImpl already has access to EventBus, this is a one-line change. Alternatively, since the agent's JiraTool also calls addComment(), it could emit the event after a successful result. Either path closes the gap between the EventBus contract and the actual state-update path.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. JiraServiceImpl.addComment() (lines 315–358) returns a ToolResult but never calls eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(...)). The only production code that flips jiraCommentPosted=true is markJiraCommentPosted() called from ShareTab.handlePostComment(). The EventBus handler in HandoverStateService at line 319 is therefore dead in production — it can only be exercised via tests. If a user posts a Jira comment via the agent's jira add_comment tool, HandoverState.jiraCommentPosted stays false. The severity is Medium, not High, because the workaround is to use the Share tab's post-comment button rather than the agent tool, and the checklist dot still turns green through the markJiraCommentPosted() path. Fixing by emitting the event from JiraServiceImpl.addComment() is safe and correct.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(key, result.data.id)) on the ApiResult.Success branch inside JiraServiceImpl.addComment(). JiraServiceImpl already holds an eventBus reference. Alternatively emit from the agent's JiraTool after a successful add_comment result.

confirmed.title: PluginSettings.aiSummariesEnabled is surfaced in HandoverConfigurable but never consulted by HandoverAiSummaryCache
confirmed.description: HandoverConfigurable exposes a checkbox 'Compute {ai.changeSummary} and {ai.ticketSummary}' bound to PluginSettings.State.aiSummariesEnabled. The setting is stored correctly. However, HandoverAiSummaryCache.changeSummary() and ticketSummary() never read this setting — they always attempt LLM generation whenever a generator EP is available. The toggle in Settings → Handover → AI summaries is therefore a dead control: disabling it has no effect on runtime behavior.
confirmed.suggestedFix: In HandoverAiSummaryCache, inject PluginSettings (add to the DI constructor). At the top of changeSummary() and ticketSummary(), check `if (!settings.state.aiSummariesEnabled) return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("AI summaries disabled")`. The test constructor already accepts injected collaborators, so adding PluginSettings there is straightforward.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverConfigurable.kt lines 110–114 bind aiSummariesEnabled to a checkbox. HandoverAiSummaryCache.changeSummary() (line 113) and ticketSummary() (line 125) call getOrCompute() unconditionally without ever reading PluginSettings.state.aiSummariesEnabled. The toggle in Settings → Handover → AI summaries is a dead control with no runtime effect. The fix is straightforward: read the setting in changeSummary()/ticketSummary() and return unavailable early. The test constructor already accepts injected collaborators so adding PluginSettings does not break any existing tests.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Inject PluginSettings into HandoverAiSummaryCache (DI constructor and test constructor). At the top of changeSummary() and ticketSummary(), add: if (!settings.state.aiSummariesEnabled) return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("AI summaries disabled").

confirmed.title: PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG keys build.planKey, build.number, automation.url silently resolve to unavailable — missing resolver branches
confirmed.description: HandoverConfigurable.PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG (core/src/.../HandoverConfigurable.kt line 185) lists 'build.planKey', 'build.number', and 'automation.url' as selectable chip keys. Users enabling these chips in Settings expect a value. HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve() (lines 70–123) only handles 'build.url' (as permanently unavailable) and has no cases for 'build.planKey', 'build.number', or 'automation.url'. All three fall to the else branch returning HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable('unknown placeholder'). The values ARE available: build.planKey and build.number come from HandoverState.buildStatus.planKey/buildNumber.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add three `when` branches to HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve(): `"build.planKey"` → `state.buildStatus?.planKey`; `"build.number"` → `state.buildStatus?.buildNumber?.toString()`; `"automation.url"` → `state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink`. Return HandoverPlaceholderValue.available(value) when non-null, or unavailable with a descriptive reason. Remove 'build.planKey', 'build.number', 'automation.url' from the catalog if they are genuinely unsupported.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverConfigurable.PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG (line 188) lists build.planKey, build.number, and automation.url as selectable chip keys.
