HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve() handles build.url (line 99, hardcoded unavailable), but has no when-branches for build.planKey, build.number, or automation.url — all three fall through to the else branch at line 121 returning 'unknown placeholder'. HandoverState does carry BuildSummary with planKey and buildNumber (lines 224–229 in HandoverStateService), so build.planKey and build.number are resolvable. automation.url could be sourced from state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink. The CLAUDE.md architecture audit (Phase 7 T-Handover-c) explicitly notes build.url is always unavailable but says nothing about the other build keys being intentionally unresolved. Fixing adds three when-branches with null-guarded available/unavailable returns, causing no regressions.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add when-branches in HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve(): build.planKey → state.buildStatus?.planKey; build.number → state.buildStatus?.buildNumber?.toString(); automation.url → state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink. Return available(value) when non-null, unavailable with a descriptive reason otherwise.

confirmed.title: CopyrightCellRenderer uses bare JLabel instead of JBLabel
confirmed.description: CopyrightCellRenderer (lines 283–286) creates `JLabel(rel, icon, ...)` and `JLabel(suffix)` instead of `JBLabel`. The project convention requires JetBrains components for all UI elements. JLabel does not respect IDE HiDPI scaling, font scaling, or theme conventions the way JBLabel does. Both labels are user-visible (file path and status suffix in the copyright file list).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace `JLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT)` with `JBLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT)` and `JLabel(suffix)` with `JBLabel(suffix)` in CopyrightCellRenderer. Add `import com.intellij.ui.components.JBLabel` and remove `import javax.swing.JLabel`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. CopyrightCellRenderer at lines 283 and 286 creates JLabel instances. The project convention requires JBLabel for all UI elements (per CLAUDE.md and the finding). The file already imports JBLabel (line 13 imports com.intellij.ui.components.JBLabel) so the fix is purely to change the two instantiation call sites. JLabel is still imported at line 39 (javax.swing.JLabel) but that import becomes unused after the fix, which is minor. The fix is trivially safe — JBLabel is a drop-in subclass of JLabel that honors IDE scaling and theming.
confirmed.recommendedAction: In CopyrightCellRenderer, change JLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT) to JBLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT) at line 283, and JLabel(suffix) to JBLabel(suffix) at line 286. Remove the now-unused javax.swing.JLabel import.

confirmed.title: HandoverStateService: no test for null activeTicket emission resetting state to blank strings
confirmed.description: Line 80: `resetForNewTicket(ticket?.key.orEmpty(), ticket?.summary.orEmpty())` is called when `activeTicketFlow` emits `null` (e.g. Stop Work). This resets `ticketId` and `ticketSummary` to blank strings. No test exercises this path. The missing coverage means a regression that, say, accidentally preserves the old ticketId after Stop Work, would go undetected.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test in `HandoverStateServiceTest`: after seeding state with a ticket, emit `null` on `activeTicketFlow` and assert that `stateFlow.value.ticketId == ""` and `stateFlow.value.ticketSummary == ""`, and that all status slices are also cleared.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed as a coverage gap but severity is Low, not High. HandoverStateService.kt line 80 calls resetForNewTicket(ticket?.key.orEmpty(), ticket?.summary.orEmpty()) when activeTicketFlow emits null. HandoverStateServiceTest has a test for a non-null ticket switch (line 308 emits TicketRef(PROJ-456)) but no test for a null emission. The behavior itself is correct — resetForNewTicket with empty strings creates a blank HandoverState. The missing test would guard against a regression that, e.g., accidentally preserves the old ticketId after Stop Work. However, this is a pure coverage issue with no currently broken behavior, so Medium or Low is more appropriate. The original High claim is inflated for a missing-test finding where the underlying code is correct.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test in HandoverStateServiceTest: seed state with a ticket, emit null on activeTicketFlow, and assert stateFlow.value.ticketId is blank, stateFlow.value.ticketSummary is blank, and stateFlow.value.copyrightFixed is false.

confirmed.title: HandoverStateService: startWorkTimestamp from settings not propagated to initial state — no test
confirmed.description: Line 66: `startWorkTimestamp = settings.state.startWorkTimestamp` seeds the initial `HandoverState` from `PluginSettings`. The existing test mocks `settings.state` but never asserts that a non-zero `startWorkTimestamp` is propagated to `stateFlow.value.startWorkTimestamp`. A regression silently zeroing the timestamp would not be caught.
confirmed.evidence:
_stateFlow.value = HandoverState(
    ticketId = initialTicket?.key.orEmpty(),
    ticketSummary = initialTicket?.summary.orEmpty(),
    startWorkTimestamp = settings.state.startWorkTimestamp
)
confirmed.suggestedFix: In `HandoverStateServiceTest.setUp()`, set `every { settings.state } returns PluginSettings.State().apply { startWorkTimestamp = 1_700_000_000_000L }`, then assert `service.stateFlow.value.startWorkTimestamp == 1_700_000_000_000L` in a dedicated test.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed as a coverage gap. HandoverStateService.kt line 66 seeds startWorkTimestamp from settings.state.startWorkTimestamp. HandoverStateServiceTest.setUp() at line 41 mocks settings.state as PluginSettings.State() with no explicit timestamp (defaults to 0). No test asserts that a non-zero startWorkTimestamp is propagated to stateFlow.value.startWorkTimestamp. The actual behavior is correct — the field is read and assigned directly. The severity is Low (pure coverage gap, no broken behavior).
confirmed.recommendedAction: In HandoverStateServiceTest.setUp(), set startWorkTimestamp = 1_700_000_000_000L on the settings state mock. Add a dedicated test asserting service.stateFlow.value.startWorkTimestamp == 1_700_000_000_000L.

confirmed.title: HandoverStateService: PullRequestCreated/JiraCommentPosted scope check when activeTicket is null — no test
confirmed.description: Lines 171-185: `checkScope` for `PullRequestCreated` compares `ctx.activeTicket?.key` (which is `null` when no ticket is active) with `event.ticketId`. When `activeTicket` is `null`, `null == event.ticketId` is `false`, so the event is correctly dropped — but this specific path has no test. A refactor changing `?.key` to `.key` (NPE) or swapping the comparison would not be detected.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test in `HandoverStateServicePrScopingTest`: clear `contextFlow.value` to `WorkflowContext()` (no active ticket), emit a `PullRequestCreated` event, and assert `expectNoEvents()` and `stateFlow.value.prCreated == false`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed as a coverage gap. HandoverStateService.checkScope() at lines 171–176 does ctx.activeTicket?.key and compares it to event.ticketId. When activeTicket is null, the comparison is null == event.ticketId which is false, so the event is correctly dropped. HandoverStateServicePrScopingTest has tests for mismatched ticketId (line 287) but not for the null-activeTicket specific path. The behavior is correct; the gap is that a null?.key refactor to .key would produce a NPE that no test would catch. Severity is Low — the logic is correct and the null case is structurally protected by Kotlin's safe-call operator.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test in HandoverStateServicePrScopingTest: set contextFlow to WorkflowContext() with no active ticket, emit PullRequestCreated with any ticketId, and assert expectNoEvents() and stateFlow.value.prCreated == false.

confirmed.title: HandoverStateService: unrecognised events (TicketChanged, BranchChanged) silently dropped — else->return branch untested
confirmed.description: Line 324: `else -> return` in `handleEvent` silently drops `TicketChanged`, `BranchChanged`, and any other event type not explicitly handled. There is no test verifying that emitting one of these events does not mutate state. While the individual happy-path tests cover that the relevant fields are updated by expected events, the no-mutation guarantee for unrecognised events is completely untested.
confirmed.evidence: else -> return // Ignore events we don't care about (TicketChanged is now handled by activeTicketFlow)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test emitting `WorkflowEvent.TicketChanged("PROJ-999", "Other")` into the event bus and asserting that `stateFlow.value` remains unchanged (e.g. `expectNoEvents()` via Turbine).
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed at line 324 of HandoverStateService.kt. The `else -> return` branch exists and is the intended handler for TicketChanged, BranchChanged, and all other unrecognised event types. Both WorkflowEvent.TicketChanged and WorkflowEvent.BranchChanged exist as real sealed-class variants. A search of HandoverStateServiceTest.kt and HandoverStateServicePrScopingTest.kt finds no test that emits either of these events and asserts `stateFlow.value` remains unchanged. The no-mutation guarantee for the `else` branch is genuinely untested. However, the behavior itself is correct and intentional — TicketChanged is explicitly routed via `activeTicketFlow`, not the EventBus path. The fix (adding a test) is safe and carries no risk of breaking anything.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test emitting WorkflowEvent.TicketChanged or WorkflowEvent.BranchChanged via the event bus and asserting expectNoEvents() (Turbine) on stateFlow. This pins the intentional silent-drop contract without changing any production code.

confirmed.title: HandoverTemplateStore: rename() method has zero test coverage
confirmed.description: Lines 230-252: `rename(id, newName)` performs a `Files.move()` and calls `rescan()`. None of the 11 tests in `HandoverTemplateStoreTest` exercise this method. The BUNDLED-rejection guard (throws `UnsupportedOperationException`), the PROJECT-origin global-clash guard (`IllegalArgumentException`), the happy-path file rename, and the `NoSuchElementException` from `findOrThrow` on an unknown id are all untested.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add three tests to `HandoverTemplateStoreTest`: (1) rename a GLOBAL template — assert old file gone, new file present, `templates.value` updated; (2) rename a BUNDLED template — assert `UnsupportedOperationException`; (3) rename a PROJECT template when GLOBAL with the same newName exists — assert `IllegalArgumentException`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading HandoverTemplateStoreTest.kt: none of the 11 tests call rename(). The rename() method at lines 230-252 of HandoverTemplateStore.kt has four distinct paths: (1) BUNDLED rejection (UnsupportedOperationException), (2) PROJECT-origin global-clash guard (IllegalArgumentException), (3) happy-path Files.move + rescan, and (4) unknown id via findOrThrow (NoSuchElementException). All four are untested. The claimed severity of High is appropriate only if rename() is a user-facing action with no UI guard — if the UI never calls rename() in practice the real-world impact is lower. Reassessing to Medium since the method is part of the public API surface and all its guards are unverified by tests. Adding the suggested tests carries no risk.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add three runTest cases to HandoverTemplateStoreTest: (1) happy-path rename of a GLOBAL template (assert old file gone, new file present, templates.value updated); (2) rename a BUNDLED template (expect UnsupportedOperationException); (3) rename a PROJECT template when a GLOBAL with the same newName already exists (expect IllegalArgumentException). Optionally add a fourth for unknown id (NoSuchElementException), covered jointly with HANDOVER-COV-6.

confirmed.title: HandoverTemplateStore: findOrThrow throws NoSuchElementException for missing id — untested for delete/update/duplicate
confirmed.description: Lines 395-397: `findOrThrow(id)` throws `NoSuchElementException` when the id is absent from `_templates.value`. This is called by `delete`, `rename`, `duplicate`, and `update`. None of the tests pass a non-existent id to any of these four methods. A caller passing a stale id (race between UI and watcher rescan) would receive an unhandled exception silently propagated inside `withContext(Dispatchers.IO)`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a `runTest` for each public mutation method (`delete`, `update`, `duplicate`) with a non-existent id string and assert `assertThrows<NoSuchElementException> { ... }`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. findOrThrow() at lines 395-397 of HandoverTemplateStore.kt throws NoSuchElementException when the id is absent. It is called by delete(), rename(), duplicate(), and update(). HandoverTemplateStoreTest.kt tests the happy paths for delete and update (and implicitly duplicate via bundled-shadow restore), but none pass a non-existent id to any of these four methods. The exception is propagated out of the withContext(Dispatchers.IO) block back to the caller. A stale id from a UI race with the watcher rescan is a realistic scenario. However, the exception is both expected and correctly typed, and callers can catch it. The gap is purely a coverage issue with no hidden wrong behavior. Severity is Low (not Medium) because the exception path is the correct documented behavior, not a silent data-loss scenario.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add runTest cases asserting assertThrows<NoSuchElementException> for delete("nonexistent"), update("nonexistent", ...), and duplicate("nonexistent", ...). Rename is covered by HANDOVER-COV-5's fourth case.

confirmed.title: HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService: 403/AUTH failure path and transient-error notification — both untested independently
confirmed.description: Lines 109-128: the auth-failure branch is triggered by `401`, `403`, or `AUTH` in the error summary. Only 401 is covered by the existing test (`live failure flips isLiveAvailable to false and notifies once`). The 403 branch and the `AUTH` substring match are not tested. The transient failure branch (lines 121-127) fires `notifyWarning` with groupId `workflow.handover.wiki.transient` — this notification call is never asserted in any test (the 5xx test only asserts `isLiveAvailable() == true`, not that the transient notification was fired).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add two tests to `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererLiveTest`: (1) 403 response flips `isLiveAvailable` to false and calls `notifyWarning` exactly once with groupId `workflow.handover.wiki`; (2) 5xx response keeps `isLiveAvailable` true and calls `notifyWarning` with groupId `workflow.handover.wiki.transient`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading HandoverWikiPreviewRendererLiveTest.kt. The existing `live failure flips isLiveAvailable to false and notifies once` test uses a 401 summary, so the 401 branch of the isAuthFailure check (line 109) is covered, but the 403 and AUTH branches (lines 110-111) are not. More significantly, the `5xx error does NOT flip isLiveAvailable to false` test (lines 120-130) only asserts `isLiveAvailable() == true` but does not verify that `notifications.notifyWarning` was called with groupId `workflow.handover.wiki.transient` (lines 122-127). A regression removing that notifyWarning call would go undetected. Neither gap involves incorrect production behavior today; both are purely coverage gaps. Severity is Medium rather than High because the 403/AUTH paths follow identical code logic to 401, and the transient-notification path has no user-visible silent-failure risk.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add two tests: (1) a 403-response test asserting isLiveAvailable() becomes false and notifyWarning is called exactly once with groupId matching `workflow.handover.wiki`; (2) extend the existing 5xx test (or add a new one) to coVerify that notifications.notifyWarning is called once with groupId `workflow.handover.wiki.transient`.

confirmed.title: HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService: requestLive when jira is null is a silent no-op — untested
confirmed.description: Line 91: `val jiraService = jira ?: return` — when the production `JiraService` EP is absent, `requestLive` is a no-op and `liveResults` never emits. The `forTest` factory accepts a non-null `JiraService`, so tests cannot exercise this path without creating a `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService` with a null jira directly.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test constructing `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService(jira = null, notifications = ..., cs = ...)` directly (use the primary constructor, not `forTest`), call `requestLive(...)`, advance until idle, and assert no `liveResults` emission and that `isLiveAvailable()` remains true.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. Line 91 of HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.kt has `val jiraService = jira ?: return` which is a documented no-op for a null JiraService. The forTest() companion factory at lines 157-165 accepts a non-null JiraService, so tests using forTest() cannot reach this branch. Constructing HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService(jira = null, ...) directly (using the primary constructor at line 30) is possible but untested. The behavior is correct and intentional. The severity is Low because: (1) in production the JiraService EP is always present; (2) the no-op is the safest possible outcome; (3) `isLiveAvailable()` correctly remains true (liveAvailable is not mutated by the early return). No production data-loss or wrong-behavior risk.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test constructing HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService(jira = null, notifications = mock, cs = scope) directly, calling requestLive(...), advancing until idle, and asserting no liveResults emission and isLiveAvailable() == true. This is low-priority but documents the null-guard contract.

confirmed.title: HandoverOverrideTracker: count30d silently ignores malformed timestamp entries — behavior untested
confirmed.description: Line 88: `runCatching { !Instant.parse(entry).isBefore(cutoff) }.getOrElse { false }` silently skips entries that are not valid ISO-8601 instants. If a settings migration or manual XML edit introduces garbage into `handoverOverrideLog`, those entries are silently excluded from the count (treated as outside the 30-day window) rather than flagged. The `getOrElse { false }` fallback is never exercised by any test.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test that manually inserts `"not-a-valid-date"` into `state.handoverOverrideLog` alongside a valid recent entry, then asserts `count30d() == 1` (only the valid entry is counted) and no exception is thrown.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. Line 88 of HandoverOverrideTracker.kt uses runCatching { }.getOrElse { false } when parsing entries. HandoverOverrideTrackerTest.kt has tests for valid-old, valid-recent, and mixed-log entries, but none insert a malformed string. The getOrElse { false } fallback treats malformed entries as outside the 30-day window (excludes them from the count), which is a safe and documented behavior — it does not throw, does not mutate state unexpectedly. The claimed severity of Medium is too high; it is Low because (a) the fallback is intentional and sensible, (b) the path is only reachable via manual XML editing or a settings migration bug, not via normal use. Adding a test for it is straightforward and harmless.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test that manually inserts a non-ISO-8601 string (e.g. "not-a-valid-date") into state.handoverOverrideLog alongside a valid recent entry, then asserts count30d() == 1 and no exception is thrown.

confirmed.title: CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile: OK (already current year) path never directly asserted
confirmed.description: Lines 144-146: `analyzeFile` returns `CopyrightFileEntry(status = CopyrightStatus.OK)` when the header already has the current year. The only `analyzeFile` test in `CopyrightFixServiceTest` asserts `YEAR_OUTDATED`. The `OK` result path — including `oldYear`/`newYear` being null — has no direct test. A regression replacing `== headerRegion` with `!= headerRegion` would invert the OK/OUTDATED classification and go undetected.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test: `service.analyzeFile("Foo.kt", "// Copyright (c) 2026 Corp\npackage foo", currentYear = 2026)` should return a `CopyrightFileEntry` with `status == CopyrightStatus.OK` and `oldYear == null` and `newYear == null`. Also add a test for `MISSING_HEADER` via a file with no copyright keyword.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed. CopyrightFixServiceTest.kt has one analyzeFile test (`analyzeFile scans 30 lines for year update`) which exercises the YEAR_OUTDATED path. Lines 144-146 of CopyrightFixService.kt return CopyrightFileEntry(status = CopyrightStatus.OK) when updated == headerRegion (header already has current year). No test covers this path. MISSING_HEADER is also not directly tested via analyzeFile (hasCopyrightHeader is tested standalone but analyzeFile's MISSING_HEADER return at lines 133-138 is not). A regression inverting the updated == headerRegion check (e.g. != instead of ==) would flip OK and YEAR_OUTDATED and go undetected. Severity is Low because (1) the OK path only occurs when the header is already correct so there is no data-loss risk, and (2) the updateYearInHeader tests thoroughly validate the year-update logic itself.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add two analyzeFile tests: (1) a file with a current-year copyright header (e.g. "// Copyright (c) 2026 Corp\npackage foo", currentYear=2026) asserting status == CopyrightStatus.OK, oldYear == null, newYear == null; (2) a file with no copyright keyword asserting status == CopyrightStatus.MISSING_HEADER.

confirmed.title: HandoverPlaceholderResolver: docker.tag when dockerTagsJson is a non-object JSON value (array/string/null literal) — untested
confirmed.description: Lines 138-143: `resolveFirstDockerTag` casts the parsed element to `JsonObject?`. If the last suite's `dockerTagsJson` is a JSON array (`"[]"`), a string literal, or `"null"`, the cast returns `null` and the resolver returns `unavailable("docker tags not a JSON object")`. This is handled gracefully but never tested. The automation module could in theory emit a malformed JSON shape from a Bamboo property.
confirmed.evidence:
val obj = json.parseToJsonElement(raw) as? JsonObject
    ?: return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("docker tags not a JSON object")
val (k, v) = obj.entries.firstOrNull()
    ?: return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("docker tags object is empty")
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add three resolver tests: (1) `dockerTagsJson = "[]"` → `docker.tag` is unavailable with reason `"docker tags not a JSON object"`; (2) `dockerTagsJson = "{}"` (empty object) → unavailable with `"docker tags object is empty"`; (3) `dockerTagsJson = "null"` → unavailable. Use an `AutomationTriggered` event with the malformed JSON to seed the state.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. HandoverPlaceholderResolver.kt lines 138-143 handle non-object JSON gracefully (safe cast to JsonObject? returns null, then returns unavailable()), but no test exercises this path. HandoverPlaceholderResolverTest.kt has tests for `docker.tag` with a well-formed object and with no suite results, but nothing for '[]', '{}' (empty object), or 'null'. The code is safe at runtime — the fallback path is correct. Fixing by adding tests is purely additive and cannot break passing behavior. Severity downgraded from Medium to Low: the edge case is fully guarded with correct fallback behavior; a malformed JSON from Bamboo is a low-likelihood production event, and the worst outcome is already handled (returns unavailable with a descriptive reason).
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add the three resolver tests suggested. They are purely additive and pin existing correct behavior.

confirmed.title: TimeTrackingService.computeElapsedHours: negative diff (currentTimestamp < startTimestamp / clock going backwards) — untested
confirmed.description: Lines 78-82: `val diffMs = currentTimestamp - startTimestamp` can be negative if the system clock rolls back (NTP correction, sleep/wake, or user error). The result is a negative elapsed hours value. There is no guard, clamp, or test for this scenario. Callers in the UI that display elapsed hours could show a negative time, and `validateHours` would reject the value (hours <= 0), but the root cause is silent.
confirmed.evidence:
fun computeElapsedHours(startTimestamp: Long, currentTimestamp: Long): Double {
    if (startTimestamp == 0L) return 0.0
    val diffMs = currentTimestamp - startTimestamp
    val hours = diffMs.toDouble() / (3600.0 * 1000.0)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test: `service.computeElapsedHours(System.currentTimeMillis() + 3_600_000L, System.currentTimeMillis())` (start in the future) returns a negative value. Separately, if the design intent is to never return negative hours, add a `coerceAtLeast(0.0)` guard and assert `computeElapsedHours(future, past) == 0.0`.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. TimeTrackingService.kt lines 77-83: `computeElapsedHours(startTimestamp, currentTimestamp)` returns an unguarded negative Double when `currentTimestamp < startTimestamp`. TimeTrackingServiceTest.kt has no test for a future startTimestamp. However, the downstream `validateHours` (line 35) rejects hours <= 0.0, so the UI path is already protected. The severity is correctly assessed as Low — a negative elapsed hours value propagates to the caller but is rejected before any Jira API call. The suggested fix (`coerceAtLeast(0.0)`) would be a valid design improvement, but is not strictly required given the downstream guard. Adding a test is purely additive and poses zero risk.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add the regression test. Optionally add `coerceAtLeast(0.0)` guard, which makes the design intent unambiguous and removes reliance on a distant downstream check.

confirmed.title: HandoverAiSummaryCache: concurrent changeSummary calls coalesce to one LLM invocation — untested
confirmed.description: Lines 165-181: `cache.computeIfAbsent` is the coalescing mechanism for concurrent callers. The comment at line 144 explicitly documents this property (`concurrent misses for the same key produce exactly one Deferred`). The existing tests only verify single-caller cache hits; no test exercises concurrent `changeSummary()` calls running simultaneously to confirm that the LLM is called exactly once under concurrent load.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test using `runTest(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())` that launches 5 concurrent `launch { cache.changeSummary() }` calls, advances until idle, and then asserts `coVerify(exactly = 1) { generator.generate(any()) }` — mirroring the pattern already used in `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererLiveTest` for the same coalescing concern.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. HandoverAiSummaryCache.kt lines 165-181 explicitly documents the coalescing contract ('concurrent misses for the same key produce exactly one Deferred') in the KDoc comment at line 144-145. HandoverAiSummaryCacheTest.kt tests single-caller cache hits thoroughly but has no concurrent multi-caller test. The D5 test (lines 339-361) is only a source-text structural pin. The `coroutineScope { computeIfAbsent { async { } } }` pattern is subtle: each concurrent caller creates a transient coroutineScope, and `ConcurrentHashMap.computeIfAbsent` atomically ensures only the first `async {}` is stored; the lambda from later callers is never executed. The second caller's `async {}` that was rejected by `computeIfAbsent` runs in the caller's `coroutineScope` but gets abandoned immediately when that scope exits. The coalescing works correctly but has no regression test. Severity remains Medium — the documented contract is critical for cost efficiency (no duplicate LLM calls) but fixing by adding tests is harmless.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a concurrent test using runTest with multiple coroutines calling changeSummary simultaneously, asserting the LLM generator is called exactly once.

confirmed.title: JiraClosureService.buildClosureComment: conflicting docker tag key across two suites — last-wins behavior untested
confirmed.description: Lines 65-76: `mergeDockerTags` iterates suites and puts each key into a `mutableMapOf`, so when two suites report the same service name with different tags, the later suite silently overwrites the earlier one. The existing merge test (`merges docker tags from multiple suites`) uses disjoint keys. The last-wins overwrite with conflicting keys is never tested; it could hide version conflicts in the QA handover comment.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a test with two suites both reporting `my-service` but with different versions (`1.0.0` and `2.0.0`). Assert that the comment contains exactly one `my-service` entry and that it is the value from the second suite (last-wins). This pins the documented behavior and makes any future change to merge semantics visible.
confirmed.verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. JiraClosureService.kt lines 65-76: `mergeDockerTags` uses `mutableMapOf` with put-on-iteration, so conflicting keys are silently last-wins. JiraClosureServiceTest.kt line 66-81 `merges docker tags from multiple suites` uses `{"my-service":"1.2.3"}` in both suites — same key AND same value — so this test passes regardless of merge semantics. The conflicting-key case (same key, different values) is never tested. The current last-wins behavior is arguably reasonable (last suite's image is the one that ran most recently), but it is undocumented and the test does not pin it. Adding a test is purely additive. Severity is Low rather than Medium because there is no wrong behavior at runtime — last-wins is a valid, deterministic policy — the gap is only that the policy is undocumented and would not surface a regression if semantics changed.
confirmed.recommendedAction: Add a test with two suites both reporting 'my-service' with different versions (e.g. '1.0.0' and '2.0.0'), asserting the comment contains exactly one 'my-service' entry with the second suite's value. This pins last-wins as the documented behavior.

rejected.title: IssueDetailCache.updateComments/updateAttachments acquire the synchronized wrapper's monitor while already holding it implicitly via the wrapper's get()
rejected.reason: The finding's core claim is wrong. `synchronized(cache)` at lines 57 and 64 explicitly locks on the `Collections.synchronizedMap` wrapper object for the ENTIRE read-modify-write sequence (`cache.get` + `cache.put`). JVM `synchronized` is reentrant, so the inner `cache[issueKey]` call (which also locks on the same object) simply re-acquires the already-held monitor — there is no deadlock and there is no TOCTOU window. While the outer `synchronized(cache)` is being held, no other thread can execute `put()` (line 52) or any other mutation on the wrapper, because all those paths also lock on the same `cache` object. The finding asserts 'there is a window where concurrent put() can interleave' — this is incorrect; the entire block is atomic under the lock. The suggested replacement (`ConcurrentHashMap.compute()`) would work, but the current `Collections.synchronizedMap` + explicit `synchronized(cache)` pattern is correct and safe. Applying the suggested fix would be a refactor with no behavioral change and carries risk of introducing a regression (e.g. losing the LRU access-order promotion that `Collections.synchronizedMap` with `LinkedHashMap(accessOrder=true)` provides, since `ConcurrentHashMap` has no access-ordered iteration).

rejected.title: QuickCommentPanel.loadedForProjectKey has a TOCTOU race: two concurrent coroutines can both bypass the early-return check and both trigger the API call
rejected.reason: The finding's premise is wrong. In QuickCommentPanel.kt the field `loadedForProjectKey` is marked `@Volatile` (line 73). More critically, the assignment `loadedForProjectKey = projectKey` is at line 121, which is BEFORE `scope.launch { ... }` at line 123. There is no suspension point between the null-check (line 120) and the assignment (line 121) — they are sequential, synchronous EDT statements. A second call to `maybeLoadVisibilityOptions` on the EDT before the coroutine is scheduled cannot slip between those two lines because the EDT is a single-threaded dispatcher. The race described (two callers both bypassing the guard before either sets the field) cannot occur as written. The suggested fix of moving the assignment before `launch` is actually already what the code does.

rejected.title: Unused public method `isValidBranchName` — no production callers
rejected.reason: Confirmed: `isValidBranchName` at line 77 of BranchNameValidator.kt has zero production callers; its only callers are in `BranchNameValidatorTest`. The CLAUDE.md MEMORY.md entry for P2 jira:F-15 explicitly notes this method is dead in production and says the correct fix is to expose it via an agent tool wrapper, NOT to delete it. The suggested fix includes deleting the corresponding tests, which would remove coverage for logic that is intentionally queued for wiring. Applying the full suggested fix (delete function + tests) would be harmful if the feature is later wired — the tests encode the validation contract and should be preserved. The method being dead in production is real, but the fix as specified (delete + remove tests) is too aggressive given the queued roadmap item.

rejected.title: JiraTaskRepository uses BaseRepositoryImpl.password field as token provider — credential may be persisted to XML by Tasks framework
rejected.reason: Not real as described. Bytecode analysis of `BaseRepository.class` from the bundled `ideaIU-2025.1.7-aarch64` tasks-core.jar (the exact IDE version targeted) shows `loadPassword()` calls `PasswordSafe.getInstance().get(getAttributes())` using `CredentialAttributes` — this is the modern PasswordSafe path, not plaintext XML serialization. The `myPassword` field is lazily populated from PasswordSafe on first `getPassword()` call. The finding's concern about 'historically stored in tasks.xml' does not apply to IntelliJ 2025.1+. The `tokenProvider = { password }` in JiraTaskRepository.kt line 33 reads from this PasswordSafe-backed field. No separate 'duplicate credential path' exists. Applying the suggested fix (overriding `getPassword()`/`setPassword()` to call `CredentialStore().getToken(ServiceType.JIRA)`) would be harmful: it would introduce a second PasswordSafe lookup for the same JIRA token, bypass the Tasks framework's own `storeCredentials()` call on UI confirmation, and potentially cause credential-not-found errors when the user configures a token via the Tasks server UI (which calls `setPassword()` on `BaseRepository`). Severity reassessed from Critical to Low — the current implementation is correct for IJ 2025.1+.

rejected.title: IssueDetailCache.updateComments and updateAttachments — race condition between synchronized(cache) block and the outer Collections.synchronizedMap wrapper not tested
rejected.reason: The claimed race condition does not exist. IssueDetailCache (lines 29-68) stores its backing map as Collections.synchronizedMap(LinkedHashMap). The synchronizedMap wrapper uses 'this' (the wrapper object itself) as its intrinsic monitor for get/put/size operations. The synchronized(cache) blocks in updateComments (line 57) and updateAttachments (line 63) also use 'cache', which IS the synchronizedMap wrapper — the same monitor object. Therefore all read-modify-write operations in updateComments/updateAttachments hold the same lock as simple put/get calls that go through the wrapper. There is no split-monitor race. The suggestion to add a concurrent stress test with runBlocking + Dispatchers.Default is also problematic per project conventions: kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking is banned in main sources by the pre-commit hook, and the suggestion to use it in tests for a PROJECT-level @Service that requires a real IntelliJ Project instance is architecturally questionable. The existing tests (IssueDetailCacheTest) correctly verify the LRU eviction contract. Fixing a non-existent race could introduce confusion or unnecessary complexity.

rejected.title: LatestBuildLookupImpl and ChainKeyResolverImpl look up BambooServiceImpl by concrete class, bypassing the service interface
rejected.reason: Not a real architecture violation — this is intentional, documented design. `BambooServiceImpl.client` is declared `internal` with KDoc explicitly stating it is 'Exposed as `internal` so that sibling classes in `:bamboo` (notably `LatestBuildLookupImpl`, the EP wired into the cross-module workflow context) can perform thin Bamboo lookups without going through the heavyweight `getLatestBuild` mapper.' The KDoc names `LatestBuildLookupImpl` as the intended consumer. `LatestBuildLookupImpl` and `ChainKeyResolverImpl` both live in the same `:bamboo` Gradle module, so `internal` access is the correct Kotlin mechanism. The tests themselves mock `BambooServiceImpl::class.java` and `bambooService.client` (as seen in `LatestBuildLookupImplTest`), confirming this is the intended test seam. Applying the suggested fix (adding a method to the `:core` `BambooService` interface) would introduce a heavyweight method for what is currently a thin client-access pattern and could break the clean API boundary between client access and service logic. Harmful to fix: the current design is not accidental; changing it would enlarge the `:core` interface for purely cosmetic architectural reasons.

rejected.title: BuildDashboardPanel reads git repository state (currentRevision, remotes) without a readAction guard from a background coroutine
rejected.reason: The finding overstates the problem and misidentifies the root cause. `GitRepository.currentRevision` and `repo?.remotes` are NOT IntelliJ PSI data — they are git4idea's own cached state properties backed by `GitRepoInfo`, protected by git4idea's own locking (not the IntelliJ read/write lock). The IntelliJ `readAction { }` convention documented in `core/CLAUDE.md` applies specifically to PSI access (ModuleUtilCore, ModuleRootManager, FileEditorManager) and module-system queries, NOT to `GitRepository` property reads. Evidence: `WorkflowContextService.onBranchChanged()` (lines 203–208 in WorkflowContextService.kt) accesses `activeRepoGit?.currentBranchName` WITHOUT `readAction` — same pattern — and this is in the core service with no issue. The `readAction` in `TicketBranchLocator` wraps the `GitRepositoryManager.getInstance().repositories` list lookup, not `currentRevision` itself. The method name `readActionLocalHead()` is misleading (implies a guard that isn't there), but that is a naming issue at Low severity, not a threading correctness bug. The finding's suggestion to wrap `currentRevision` in `readAction` would not cause harm but also provides no safety benefit for this data type.

rejected.title: CoveragePreviewPanel makes a SonarDataService.getLineCoverage suspend call from a plain Dispatchers.IO scope without injecting the suspend call through a proper IO context
rejected.reason: The finding itself concludes 'no structural change needed for the scope itself' and explicitly states the actionable issue is covered by SONAR-ARC-1 (Dispatchers.Main → Dispatchers.EDT). The scope allocation pattern (CoroutineScope + SupervisorJob + Dispatchers.IO, cancelled in dispose()) is the project-accepted pattern for non-@Service Disposable UI components — the CLAUDE.md notes this pattern is used by SonarDataService and the architecture section calls out the Disposer chain. The `Dispatchers.Main` usage at lines 115 and 131 is a real convention deviation (should be Dispatchers.EDT), but that is a separate finding (SONAR-ARC-1) that is not listed here. As stated in the finding's own suggested fix, there is nothing actionable here beyond what ARC-1 covers.

rejected.title: QueueEntryStatus.TRIGGERING is never set and creates dead branches in QueueStatusPanel
rejected.reason: The finding is correct that TRIGGERING is never assigned by QueueService in production and the when-arms at QueueStatusPanel.kt lines 144 and 161 are unreachable at runtime. However, the suggested fix of removing TRIGGERING from the enum is HARMFUL and must not be done. TagHistoryService.kt line 267 uses QueueEntryStatus.valueOf(rs.getString('status')) to deserialize persisted SQLite rows. If any user has a row with status='TRIGGERING' in their on-disk DB, removing the enum value causes IllegalArgumentException at IDE startup — exactly the same trap as TAG_INVALID (which was documented and retained for the same reason at AutomationModels.kt lines 69-75). The MEMORY.md explicitly records 'automation:F-11 delete TRIGGERING = HARMFUL — valueOf() startup crash; do NOT'. AutomationModelsTest line 65 also asserts TRIGGERING exists and line 76 asserts statuses.size == 9 — removing it would break a test. The 4th fallback in pickActiveEntry is load-bearing for potential TRIGGERING rows recovered from SQLite. Only the unreachable when-arms in QueueStatusPanel are safe to address, not the enum value itself.

rejected.title: loadGeneration counter mutated from both EDT and IO dispatcher without @Volatile
rejected.reason: The finding claims line 659 reads loadGeneration on Dispatchers.IO, but inspection of the actual code shows line 658 is invokeLater { and line 659 is if (token != loadGeneration) { — the read is inside the invokeLater lambda, executing on the EDT. Similarly every other read of loadGeneration in the file (lines 475, 496, 591) is inside an invokeLater block. All writes (lines 327, 382, 463, 1149) are also on the EDT. Every access — both reads and writes — happens on the EDT. There is no cross-thread read of a non-volatile field. The finding is based on an incorrect reading of the code structure (assuming the read at 659 is on IO because reconcileFromBamboo runs on IO, when in fact it is inside an invokeLater nested call that defers to the EDT). Adding @Volatile would be harmless but unnecessary; the JVM memory model does not require it here.

rejected.title: AutomationTriggered event carries full dockerTagsJson payload on EventBus
rejected.reason: The finding's central claim — 'no known subscriber currently uses the raw JSON value from the event' — is factually wrong. HandoverStateService.kt:243-260 explicitly consumes WorkflowEvent.AutomationTriggered and stores event.dockerTagsJson into SuiteResult.dockerTagsJson (line 260), which is then used by HandoverPlaceholderResolver.kt:107/131/135 to resolve {{docker.tag}} and {{docker.tagsJson}} placeholders, by JiraClosureService.kt:69 to format Jira closure comments, and by ChecksTab.kt:207 to display deployed tags in the handover UI. Removing or reducing dockerTagsJson to a serviceCount integer would break the entire handover docker-tag display and Jira closure workflow. The sensitivity concern in the comment at TagBuilderService.kt:280-281 refers to the idea.log, not the EventBus — there is no evidence that any hook system or memory path logs or persists the event payload. The finding is not real as an information-disclosure risk, and the suggested fix is harmful.

rejected.title: CopyrightFixService.consolidateYears: inverted year range (end < start) expands to a huge set — untested
rejected.reason: The claimed behavioral bug does not exist. Verified by running the actual Kotlin code: consolidateYears("2025-2020", 2026) returns "2020-2026", not "2025-2026" or "2025-currentYear" as the finding implies. Here is why: YEAR_RANGE_PATTERN matches "2025-2020" with start=2025, end=2020; allYears.addAll(2025..2020) adds nothing because IntRange(2025, 2020).isEmpty() == true. However, YEAR_PATTERN.findAll("2025-2020") then adds both 2025 and 2020 individually as separate matches (the "-" is not a word character, so \b boundaries are satisfied on both sides). Therefore allYears = {2025, 2020}, min = 2020, and the result is "2020-2026" — the correct earliest-year consolidation. The production code handles inverted ranges correctly by falling back to individual year extraction. The coverage gap (no test for this path) is real but the claimed severity of Medium based on a silent wrong-output rewrite is not supported by the code's actual behavior.
